string.text: I don't know. Feels like I need it, but my gut says I shouldn't.
I don't know. This isn't my first day in the Zone, and taking something like that right away is risky.
Push that on some green rookie. I'm not convinced yet.
The Zone's too dangerous a place to grab just anything. I need to think.
Pal, this isn't my first year in the Zone... I'm not taking it for now. Maybe later.
Build yourself a reputation first. Buying from you just like that feels shady.
I can see everyone's hustling however they can. I don't need it right now. Feels shady.
I don't know. Feels like I need it, but my gut says I shouldn't.
I'd take it, but something about it feels off.
I'll pass for now. You never know.
Not taking it for now. My own stuff is more reliable.
We're supplied as it is. Is it even worth it...
You should try it on yourself first. Who knows. I'll pass for now.
We're already supplied. Unless it's dirt cheap...
We've got our own. I'd need to think about it. Depends on the price.
We have our own supply lines, but I'll think about your offer.
Cool it, pal, we've got enough of our own. Come back later, we'll talk.
This is Duty. We've got almost everything here. Come back later, we'll see.
We're fully supplied. Extra stock never hurts, of course. Come back later.
Wouldn't be bad to have in reserve. I'm not taking it right now. I'll think on it.
We're supplied. Maybe we'll take yours too, but not right now.
If you'd offered some weed, that'd be different. Come back later.
Better dump that expired crap on Duty. Feels shady.
I'll think about it, but bring something to clear the head while you're at it.
If we can agree on the price, I might take it.
Started doing business, have you? Good for you, but I'm not buying just yet.
You should've offered some weed instead. Though this wouldn't hurt either, of course. I'll think about it.
This is Freedom. Everything's sold freely here, if we can agree on the price...
You're late, we're already buzzing.
You should've offered vodka, but your stuff might do for a morning fix.
Bring vodka next time. Though this won't hurt either.
All right, pal, lay out the whole lot. Maybe then we'll talk.
Don't try to sell me garbage. Give me a real price, then we'll talk.
I thought you'd offer something for a buzz. I'll think about it.
Pal, a little later.
Don't bust my brains. I don't need it right now.
We've already got pills. If yours are better, we'll talk.
Your business doesn't fly here. Drop the price, then we'll talk.
Pal, a little later.
Don't bust my brains. I don't need it right now.
No... why would I? I use traditional remedies.
Why would I need it? Ignat can get proper remedies.
Offer that to stalkers over there. We use our own.
We don't need it, thanks...
I've got our own hunters' remedies.
No thanks, nothing beats our own remedies.
Just between us girls, your medicine is crap.
Why would I need it? Ignat can get proper remedies.
I don't know. Feels like I need it, but my gut says I shouldn't.
I don't know. This isn't my first day in the Zone, and taking something like that right away is risky.

I've heard of those, and I'd rather not have. Once one of those trucks rolled into a town, and afterward everybody there died out, every last one. Now they call that place the Dead City. That's how it is. Another truck was seen somewhere near the Red Forest, and after that people started disappearing there. Whoever goes there never comes back...

Those trucks are omens of trouble... If you ever see one, take it as the first bell ringing, and the last one too. That's the Zone telling you you'll be riding in one of those trucks soon enough... Thank God I haven't seen a single one yet. You have, have you? Then that's it, you're done for, and I'm not talking about this anymore.

Get lost with your trucks! Somebody else asked around about them too, and where is he now? See that cross over there? Go say hello. You'll be there soon enough if questions like that interest you, and then you can ask him... No, don't even ask me.

People say all kinds of things. Mostly nonsense. Stalkers are superstitious, everybody has their own hang-ups. And this topic's taboo for everyone, nobody likes talking about it. It's nonsense, sure... though who knows. There must be something to it. Rumors don't spring up out of nowhere. So I'd better keep my mouth shut too. You never know...

"Death trucks," you mean? Knock on wood... God forbid I ever meet one. They say it's worse than the Black Mark: see one once, and you're bound to end up in one. I don't even want to talk about them...

The ones with corpses in them? That's some question... Like there aren't any better topics. Don't mention them again. That's like talking about the Grim Reaper dropping by for you today... I don't even want to think about it.

I've heard about them in passing, but the men don't like talking about them. There's something terrifying about them. Someone even said they haul half-dead zombies around the Zone. Zombies behind the wheel too. It picks a spot, dumps them in a heap, and then they crawl all over the place like roaches... Filthy business...

What? I wouldn't wish meeting one of those trucks on my worst enemy. Anyone who sees one gets this stubborn urge to come back and "take a ride" in it. Like some kind of pull. And what it carries is corpses. So if you spot one from a distance, give it a wide berth.

You mean the corpse wagons? Someone said they're self-propelled, like radio-controlled. Like the scientists came up with that shit so they wouldn't have to run around the Zone themselves... always studying something... nerds. One guy even said they recruit volunteers and take them for a ride in that truck, but nobody survives the trip... All they care about is studying things, for fuck's sake! People die because of their research and they couldn't care less.

I heard the scientists have teamed up with the military and are running experiments like that. They load up corpses and zombies, then drive them around the Zone. After each corpse they cut it into pieces and study how the Zone affected it... And if they don't have enough for their report, they shoot stalkers or zombify them to fill the truck. Watch yourself around the scientists... they could shove anybody into that truck.

There's a rumor somebody collects organs that way, then smuggles them outside the Zone. They load up fresh corpses, still warm, and spirit them away. Former stalkers do the loading, the kind that don't give a damn about anything anymore as long as there's money in it... Fuckers! And if you stumble across them during one of those harvests, they'll plug you without a second thought and load you up too. For cash they'd kill their own mother, the bastards!

People saw a truck like that once. Weird mystical shit. It came from God knows where and God knows how. Rolled in, stopped... and silence. Then it vanished just as mysteriously. But the strangest part was that there was nobody at the wheel... Or maybe there was, only invisible, like a ghost or something. And in the back, probably the souls of those killed in the Zone too... Gives me gooseflesh just thinking about it...

They say one of them drove through here. Nobody really knows where from, but the men say it's a sign. Means it's time to get out. It's a "death truck." You get the meaning? DEATH. Only it was empty. And why was it empty? Because it came here looking to stock up on corpses... It's no good, not good at all...

Strange little trucks. They don't even look real... Just another incomprehensible thing, like everything else in this Zone. My advice is don't go near them. They're like anomalies, another spawn of the Zone. I can't even imagine what they are... I only know that anything you can't make sense of in the Zone is best avoided if you want to stay alive.

There's a strange link with them. One of those trucks shows up, then disappears... Seems like nothing, but at the same time reports of stalkers dying start pouring in. I'm sure there's some connection. Like it takes the number of deaths it needs and disappears somewhere... along with those poor bastards. That's how it is. God forbid one of them shows up here.

Ever heard of Семецкий [TERM?]? They say he used to be a driver, and now he rides around the Zone in one of those trucks. He appears in that truck out of nowhere, then disappears into nowhere. And when word comes in that Семецкий [TERM?] has died, it means one of those trucks vanished somewhere. That's supposed to mean good luck, that you can head out and won't run into a "death truck." Probably nonsense... What's Семецкий [TERM?] got to do with it anyway?..

Those aren't trucks at all. They're alien transport. That's one theory. They came from space and they're studying us. They just disguise themselves that way so they don't spook us, their test subjects. Though that only makes them scarier... They'd be better off flying around in saucers, at least then it'd be clearer who they are and what they want.

It's all nonsense and stalker tall tales. "Death truck"... Nice name, I'll give them that. One guy spun some bullshit and everybody keeps repeating it and trembling: "God forbid, God forbid"... You're just another one who bought those stories about the trucks. It's all bullshit.

Another one. It's a joke, don't you get it? "A little boy climbed into the cab..." Or however it goes... Anyway, "It started up soft, rolled away soft, and a rookie stalker found a hundred corpses." Something like that. Long story short, it's just a gag. Don't take it to heart. Sit down, have a drink, grab a bite, relax.

Everybody says "death truck." But they aren't omens of approaching death, they are death itself. The Zone is God's punishment for everything people have done. And the truck is His message, in a form a man can understand, that death has come and it is inevitable... If you see a truck, don't waste time on foolishness. Pray, and accept what He has in store for you with a light heart.

Those aren't ordinary trucks, they're hearses. And they don't foretell anything, no matter what people say here. If you saw a hearse in an ordinary city, would you think right away, "It's come for me"? Calm down. It's taking someone to be buried, not you. And these trucks have just as little to do with you. It came, it went... what's it to you?

There's no point trying to understand something that's unknowable by nature. Nobody understands what an anomaly is, how it appears, how it disappears. Nobody knows where artifacts come from... These trucks are the same kind of thing. Nobody knows anything and nobody ever will. All that'll keep going around are tales and horror stories about them. What else is left, if there's no way to make sense of it? All people can do is make up stories.

Back when it all first blew, the army and the administration hired truck drivers to haul out the bodies. A sea of people died back then. They hauled them out of Припять [TERM?], out of Лиманск [TERM?], gathered them from the villages. There weren't any anomalies yet, so they tried to pull people out, if only so they could be buried properly, and all that... That's why the drivers themselves started calling their vehicles corpse wagons. But that was a long time ago... and people are still talking.

Who the hell knows. They say there are vehicles hauling stiffs straight through anomalies. I haven't seen it myself, just heard stories. This is the Zone, buddy. You could see flying mammoths racing crocodiles here...
Ah, to hell with them. They say death trains roll along the broken rails over at Янов [TERM?], and you're asking me about trucks... Ask me about airplanes while you're at it.
What trucks? You hardly ever walk upright here, you spend most of your time conquering space on your belly...

I'll tell you this: don't stick your nose into questions that aren't yours. The fact you're asking means you aren't afraid, because you don't know anything. I know a little, and I'm afraid. So let's drop it.

It's all too strange. They say those things always roam at night. Nobody's seen or heard them by day, but nights are different. It's like the Flying Dutchman: nothing but dead men in those machines, or so I've heard...

Is anything here known for certain at all? Even the scientists don't know, and you're asking me. Ask those nerds sometimes and whatever you bring up, all you get is "blah blah blah" and a long runaround, and in the end their answers are worth nothing. Freeloaders.
That's how they haul the nosiest stalkers out of the Zone. Once the truck bed's full, off they go! Want to join them too?
People say all sorts of things. A rumor came through not long ago that there really are trucks like that. Someone from one of them is even living on the Cordon. Go ask around there.

I think those trucks are basically garbage trucks. Somebody in the center is making or producing something, and they haul all the trash away. Sometimes they carry crates, there are enough of those all over the Zone, sometimes zombies, which are trash too, used-up material... Where else would all this come from?
What Ghost? We run into dead men getting back up around here, and you...
Ghost? Nah... I don't remember anyone like that.
What Ghost? Believe fewer tall tales... I don't know anything.
Who? A real ghost? And you believe that? Bullshit... Though in the Zone, you can run into just about anything.
People said something about him, I just don't remember who... I think they were talking about him in the Bar. Can't recall.
Ghost? Our boys knew him, they even drank together. Bandits killed him. Or no... I'm mixing him up, that was Фантом [TERM?]. I don't know Ghost.
I've heard of him, but nothing specific. He was here once, but he didn't show off, didn't put himself on display, and generally tried to stay in the shadows and go unnoticed. A ghost, in a word.

Yeah, yeah... funny you should mention him now. I keep thinking who you remind me of... You look a bit like him. Or maybe not him, maybe his companion... It was a long time ago, hard to remember now, and nobody's talked about Ghost himself in ages. I don't really know anything, where he is, what he's up to... But you do resemble him somehow.

Ghost was here once, though it was a long time ago. And he wasn't alone, he had some oddball with him. They kept asking about some kind of metal contraptions. We don't know anything about that stuff, so nobody remembered much. They hung around here a little while, then slipped off somewhere.

Somebody told me he came with some medic, or a doctor. They were looking for medicine or something like that... Seems somebody got caught in an anomaly... I don't know myself, I'm just repeating what I heard.

How could I not know Ghost? Me and the other stalkers talked with him, just like I'm talking with you now. Sure, we were drinking then, but not so much that I'd forget. Most of the talking was between us, though... heh-heh. He was the quiet type, just nodding along to everything. Then he disappeared somewhere. But we had other things on our minds by then. Haven't seen him since.

Ghost? He's one of the founders of the stalker trade. A stalker patriarch, if you can call him that. For a while people kept saying he'd walked almost the whole Zone. Just like they said about Strelok. Maybe they're even the same man. But nobody talks about him anymore. Guess people talked it to death, or everybody got tired of hearing about some legendary stalker.

People said Ghost walked the Zone with Strelok... or maybe still does. It's just that people haven't been saying much about them lately. There was a time when everybody thought it was their duty to tell some story about how they'd practically walked to the center of the Zone with Ghost and Strelok. Lies, of course. I can't even remember what those stories were about now, but back then they were interesting to listen to. What else were you supposed to do with no radio and no TV?

They say there is no Ghost, that it's all just stories. But there are people like that, the kind who know everything, can do everything, have some business of their own that only they understand, and stay so unnoticed that nobody even believes they're real. Like ghosts, they come and go. I'm telling you, there is a stalker called Ghost. I just don't know where to look for him.

They saw him in the Bar once. I didn't see him myself, but they said the whole place suddenly started whispering, "Oh! Ghost! Ghost!" And everyone tried to come up to him on one excuse or another: "Got a smoke?", "Know what time it is?", "Could you tell me how to get to the library?" Everybody practically wanted to touch a living Ghost. That's how it was. Then he hung around a bit and left. I was too late, I'd already missed him.

We were sitting by the campfire once when a stalker-looking fellow walked up. He sat down and started talking to one of our guys. I think they knew each other. They kept muttering and muttering. Most of our lot weren't paying much attention, but I heard the name Ghost. Big name, so I started listening, and I didn't understand a damn thing. They kept talking about some kind of generator. Maybe they needed a gas generator or something? And what does Ghost have to do with that? Then they got up, walked off somewhere, and that was it.

The name's well known. But I can't tell you anything specific. Ghost wouldn't be Ghost if everybody knew where his hideout was and how to find him. That's why he's Ghost: nobody knows anything concrete about him.

Ghost... Strelok... that's all anybody ever talks about, like the rest of the stalkers are somehow worse. No, see, Ghost's a stalker! And me or you, we're nothing. Not worth spit. I don't even want to talk about him.
Seems Ghost had dealings with the scientists. Either he worked for them, or he was one of them. Ask them.

I heard the military were looking for him. Somebody said the army ran a whole operation in the Zone, hunting some ghosts or shapeshifters. And they were looking for this Ghost too, seeing as his name stands out so much. Maybe they found him, maybe he went to ground somewhere, since nobody says anything about him anymore.

A lot of people say Ghost is a stalker sent by God. Nonsense. He's an ordinary man. He just got lucky and found something very unusual. Most people don't. That's normal. Stalking's like a lottery, there can't be many winners. Ghost just happens to be one of the lucky ones.
Ghost? Nah... I don't remember anyone like that.
What Ghost? I don't know anybody with a nickname like that.
People said something about him, I just don't remember who... I think they were talking about him. Can't recall.
I heard something about Ghost. Just idle chatter. All I know is he wasn't exactly close with our crowd...
One man's looking for Ghost, another one's after Strelok... What is this, an information desk?
Get lost! Go ask the passport office... Heh!
Somebody was already looking for him. Why do you keep asking around here? None of our boys goes by that.
It's a striking name... "Ghost" really has a nice ring to it. Must be one hell of a man to have a nickname like that. But I don't know him.
Why are you on my ass? There are people all over the Zone. Today one proudly calls himself Орёл [TERM?], tomorrow he's Пыжик [TERM?]... Yesterday he was Ghost, today he's Ghoul... Go find him.
Hell if I know. Maybe we crossed paths sometime. How are you supposed to remember everyone?

Did he do something to you, for you to be asking around? You know Телега Сопливый [TERM?]? I do. But on the PDA he shows up as Олег Бритва [TERM?], there you go. So how does your Ghost show up, do you know? Exactly...
I know him! We were drinking last week, my head's still splitting. Hell if I know where he is now. No... wait, that one wasn't called Ghost, was he? Damn, I'm an idiot... I can't remember anymore.
Think I saw a stalker like that at Agroprom recently. Look around there.

We were hanging around here the other day. I don't remember who said it exactly, but there was talk that some guys came back from beyond Барьер [TERM?] with artifacts so good each of them flew home in his own helicopter. Лёха Лысый [TERM?], Дюха Призрак [TERM?], and Витёк Снайпер [TERM?]. They're probably kicking back somewhere in Мальдивы [TERM?] now... Ghost too, I guess.

Haven't seen him myself, but I heard about one like that. Him and Ремень [TERM?] sold a live Pseudogiant to the scientists. Made a fortune. Probably bullshit, though. Try catching one alive yourself...

Hell if I know which one you're asking about. Андрюха Призрак [TERM?] used to hang around somewhere in Garbage. Серёга Призрак [TERM?] was skulking around Росток [TERM?], and Мишка Призрак [TERM?] wanders around Yantar. Which one do you need?

A stalker walks into the Bar with an ostrich and a wet cat. Everyone's surprised, but says nothing. He orders a bottle of vodka, drinks it straight from the neck, and heads for the dark far end of the bar. He sits there and cries. The Barkeep comes over and asks what's wrong.\n- You see, I was at the CNPP, climbed into the sarcophagus, and found a wish granter in there.\nLooks like I'd gone completely crazy without female company, because I wished there would always be a chick with long legs and a wet pussy right beside me.

Two down-on-their-luck thugs are sitting by the campfire, dreaming:
 - Ah, fuck, if only we could hobble all the way to the Monolith. Then I'd really make it big! Hell, I'd wish for... five grand in rubles!
 Pause. His companion asks in confusion:
 - Listen, pal, maybe... six grand, huh?
 Another pause. Then the reply:
 - No, are you crazy?! Six grand?! That's IMPOSSIBLE!

Agroprom. Some thugs are walking toward the stalkers' base.\n - I ran off to the side for a second and saw a stalker's grave. You know what it says on the cross, boys?\n"Killed defending the rear entrance."

A veteran stalker is lecturing the rookies:
 - Right then! Stick together, stand by each other, don't screw your comrades over, don't go putting every filthy thing in your mouth... I had one "partner" here once, the bastard. Bandits caught us, lined us up against the wall, and started joking around: choose, they said, either please us, or we shoot you.
 - So what did you say? ask the rookies.
 - What did we say? That piece of shit agreed, and I... I got shot.
A thug, drunk out of his mind, is standing by a wall when he suddenly drops to the floor. He lies there and says:\n - Fuck, boys, that wall is definitely trying to crush me. I'm telling you.
- Buddy, help me! came a shout to the stalker as he was walking past some bushes.\n - What, you want a medkit? Hah! Bastard!\n - Naaah... I ran out of paper!
A tired, blood-soaked stalker in an exoskeleton with a grenade launcher is dragging a pseudofish. Another one comes toward him:\n - Hey, pal! How's the fishing?\n - Painful, damn it.

A loner and a Freedom member meet one day. The loner asks him for a smoke, and the Freedom guy hands him a cigarette. The loner lights up and says:
 - Hmm, this doesn't taste much like "Беломор [TERM?]"?!
 The Freedom member replies:
 - You'll laugh for a long time, but this isn't "Беломор [TERM?]".

A black guy from the States came to the Zone to hunt mutants. He brought a top-notch gun and a top-notch suit with him. A day later they brought him to an ICU in Kyiv. The doctor asks him, "What happened?" He says, "Well, I ran out of water. And it was already night. I walked up to a tent, stuck my hand with the flask in there, and said: water... please give me some water!"

A hunchbacked stalker is walking through Кровососовка [TERM?]. Suddenly, a voice in his head says: "Got a smoke, stalker?"
Now he nearly craps himself and thinks: "Please don't touch me, I'm little, hunchbacked, and I don't smoke."
 The voice again: "No, you're not hunchbacked."
 Then the voice fades away...
 The stalker runs back to base, up to a mirror, and the hump is gone.
 So he goes to his lame friend and tells him there's this kind Controller in Кровососовка [TERM?]. 
 The friend heads there. Walks through the village. The voice says: "Hey, hunchback, got a smoke?"
 "No, I'm not hunchbacked. That stalker before me, he was the hunchback. I'm lame."
 "No, you're hunchbacked!"

Two stalkers from Freedom decided to contribute to the study of the Zone. They bring Professor Sakharov a report on their experiments over at Yantar. He reads:
 Experiment one: Caught a tushkano, whistled, the tushkano ran away.
 Experiment two: Caught a tushkano, tore its legs off, whistled, the tushkano did not run away.
 Conclusion: A tushkano without legs can't hear.

- Why's Vasyan so jumpy?
 - Because of an artifact...
 - What artifact?
 - You don't know? Vasyan found a really rare artifact. It's called "Сердце друга [TERM?]." It's in two parts. You keep one for yourself and give the other one away. After that, everything gets shared between you: joy, pain, sorrow!
 - So what?
 - He's on his period.

The stalkers are sitting by the campfire, warming up, bored. Another one walks over:
 - Hey, boys! Why are you sitting here staring at the sky? - he pulls a bundle from his backpack. - Here, check out the dope. The stalkers perk up and start rubbing their hands. It goes around the circle:
 - Oh, excellent!
 - S-s-s-so gooood!
 - Nah, kind of average.
 - I've had better.
 - What are you talking about, boys? It's nothing but wood chips. I didn't feel a thing! says the rookie.
 - There you go, you ruined the whole buzz, you idiot! You're in the Zone! Is it that hard to imagine it's dope and not sawdust?!

- Кандёха [TERM?], fuck this Zone. Let's head back to the neighborhood and hustle!
 - No-o-o, I need to get to the Monolith. I've got one personal wish...
 - A half-arm-long dick?
 - Fuck... how the hell did you know?!
A boar is chasing a Flesh and thinking:\n - Can Sidorovich really run faster than me?

Some rookies are sitting by the campfire, dreaming:
 - Ah, once we move up a little, run some jobs, and make some cash. We'll buy ourselves top-notch gear, tricked-out guns, and head North. They say the artifacts there are worth their weight in gold. We'll get rich, get out of the Zone, buy some real cars, and go relax in the Канары [TERM?], over there in Таити [TERM?]. Ever been to Таити [TERM?]?
 Suddenly a Bloodsucker leaps at the rookies, grabs them by the ears, and smashes their heads together hard. Skulls crack. The Bloodsucker licks his lips:
 - Таити [TERM?], Таити [TERM?]! We've never been to any Таити [TERM?]! They feed us just fine right here!
A thug walks past a wounded stalker.\n Stalker: Help me, pal!\n Bandit: No offense, buddy, I've only got one magazine left.

Attention everyone! Strange zombies have appeared in the Zone, floating along Припять [TERM?] in a kayak. The kayak usually contains two mysterious beings calling themselves Гребибля [TERM?] and Гребубля [TERM?]... And sometimes there's a third, no less mysterious creature called Кудатудабля [TERM?].

A stalker who's completely wasted crawls up to the bar and says to the Barkeep:\n - Hey, friend, put on that Валерий Меладзе [TERM?] song about Стоша Говнозад [TERM?] for the boys! - What are you talking about? There's no such song! says the Barkeep, surprised.\n - How is there not? "Стоша Говнозад [TERM?], тихо на пальцах..."

A stalker buys a bottle of vodka from the Barkeep and tries to open it, but can't.\n - Hey, Barkeep! Why won't your bottle open?\n - It's elementary! Find a "Crystal Thorn," pierce the cork through a "Pellicle," hook the other end onto a "Spring," stick three "Batteries" into the "Spring," press it, and the bottle will open!

A veteran stalker is sitting in the bar having a drink, and two rookies are sitting nearby. One of them keeps looking enviously at the stalker's snack and sighing, so the stalker says:
 - Hey, tell your buddy to come join us, we'll sit a while.
 The second replies:
 - No, he's full.
 - Then let him stop pissing himself and come have a drink.
A rookie is walking through the Zone at night. Suddenly something starts chirping.\n - A cricket, the rookie thinks.\n - Radiation, idiot, says the Controller sitting in the bushes.

- You hear about the incident at Duty's base?
 - So what happened to them?
 - A Controller got right into the radio room. Spent half an hour recruiting for "Грех [TERM?]."
 - What's the point?
 - Duty members are straightforward guys: if one of their own is recruiting, then it must be the right thing to do. They spent the whole day looking for this "Грех [TERM?]" so they could join it.

One cheerful Controller in the Zone decided to check what would happen if he made a Bloodsucker drink a zombie's blood. In the end both died: the Bloodsucker from hunger, the zombie from being tickled...

A veteran stalker wound up in Hell, shrugged off his heavy gear, threw away his battered rifle, dropped to his knees, raised his hands up, and joyfully shouted at the top of his lungs: "Hura-a-a-a-ay, at last, the long-awaited rest!"
Geneticists tried to combine a Chimera's invulnerability, a Bloodsucker's feeding method, and a Controller's abilities. That's how they recreated the mother-in-law!

An old Bloodsucker is teaching a young one:\n - Don't go to the Cordon. The rookie stalkers there don't have money for anti-rad meds. They "treat" themselves with vodka. I ate one once and spent two days sick with a hangover afterward...

A zombie, dying:
 - Piiiiss....
 A stalker walks past:
 - Well, damn... People ask for water when they're dying, and this one...
A Controller and three stalkers are sitting by the campfire.\n Controller: "All right, the moment I wave my hand, everybody laughs".

A rookie asks a veteran stalker:
- You don't happen to know anywhere around here without pseudodogs? I'm afraid to sleep.
Veteran:
- Look, go straight ahead, then turn onto the road, then left, and you'll see a little village. You won't need to be afraid of dogs there.
Rookie:
- Great! So why aren't there any?
- They're afraid of Bloodsuckers.
There once were three stalker brothers. Two were smart, and the third was a fool: one day he'd take a shit in a "Whirligig," the next he'd piss in an "Electro"...

The tax inspector comes to see Sidorovich. They knock on the iron door:
 - Does Sidorovich live here?
 - No!
 - Then who are we speaking to?
 - Sidorovich.
 - Then why are you saying he doesn't live here?
 - You call this living?

On a dark, rainy night, a stalker is sneaking through the Zone and stumbles across one of his own, dozing under the open sky, with a rusty bus not far away.\n - Hey, buddy, why didn't you spend the night in the bus?\n - I did, but then a Controller kicked me out...

Two Freedomers are coming back from a hemp field. One suddenly spots a snail and stomps it with savage fury.\n - What did it ever do to you, pal?! asks the other.\n - It drove me fucking crazy, chasing me all day!

The Barkeep is talking to the Swamp Doctor.
 - You don't know why Voronin's hair is so thick?
 - He rubs eggs into it.
 - Well, you're an acrobat...
A stalker walks into Sidorovich's cellar just as two men are robbing the old man. He rushed to the rescue...\n The haul had to be split three ways...
Stalker wisdom: "A word isn't a sparrow. Let one careless one fly out, and a whole three-story one comes flying back."

A stalker went on a run once, saw a Flesh, and took a shot at it, but missed.\n - Damn, I've gotten completely cross-eyed, he says, then glances around, sees nobody nearby, and adds:\n - Though I always was.

The Barkeep and Wolf are arguing.
 Barkeep: "A bundle of crowbars usually sinks."
 Wolf: "But it floats perfectly well in mercury."
 Barkeep: "But if the crowbars are uranium, they sink even in mercury."
 Wolf: "Then you try sinking uranium crowbars in mercury."
Only Russian and Ukrainian stalkers, hearing the words "one hundred grams," don't ask: "Of what?"

Freedomers are talking:
 - Imagine this, Vasya drank some PROPER beer at the bar yesterday and felt like he was born again!
 - No hangover?
 - No, he was crawling, pissing himself, forgot how to talk...
One stalker says to another:\n - I can hit a rat from 100 paces.\n - Interesting. Where are you going to find rats that'll wait for you to measure out a hundred paces?

There's a sign hanging on the door in Sidorovich's cellar:
 "I've got everything a real stalker needs!"
 And right below it, another one:
 "And if I don't have something, then a real stalker doesn't need it!"

Some stalkers are sitting by the campfire. One says:
 - They slipped me another pair of shitty boots.
 The second replies:
 - That's not funny.
 The first says:
 - But it's true!

An announcement over Sidor's shack:\n "New promotion! Buy two Kalashes, eight hundred rounds for them, three Makarov pistols, six magazines for each, twelve anti-rads (or sixteen bottles of vodka), eighteen medkits, and seven grenades, and get A BANDAGE ABSOLUTELY FREE!"
A stalker is sitting by the campfire strumming a single chord on his guitar.\nA Controller sits down beside him and says with a smile:\n - Well then, stalker, shall we start the concert by request?..

A stalker walks up to a Freedom checkpoint and says:
 - Why is there such a high fence around Freedom's base?
 - So fools won't climb over it.
 - Into it or out of it?
Vodka "Казаки [TERM?]" is like a Russian nesting doll: open one, and then comes the second, the third...
In Sidorovich's little cellar:\n - Sidorovich, do you have a conscience?\n - We-e-ell... How much are you offering?

Two Freedomers are sitting together:
 - Ah, the weed just isn't what it used to be...
 A snork rides past on a bicycle.
 - Looks just right to me!

Two stalkers:
 - Why do you smell so bad?
 - Well, I spotted a "Night Star," and right next to it a Pseudogiant was sleeping with its back to me, so I crawled over to get it. - And?
 - And what? The giant was having nightmares!

Garbage. A stalker and a bandit are walking toward each other. The bandit pulls out a ПМ [TERM?], points it at the stalker, and asks:
 - Got money?
 The stalker pulls out an Абакан [TERM?], points it at the bandit, and says quite calmly:
 - Why?
 The bandit puts away the ПМ [TERM?] and pulls out a thousand-ruble note:
 - Just wanted it changed!

Stalkers are sitting by the campfire... chatting... drinking... shooting the breeze...
 A rookie walks over... sits down... takes off his boots... stretches them toward the fire... everyone freezes...
 A minute of confusion... Then one of them asks:
 - Listen... pal... do you even change your socks at all?...
 And he replies:
 - Sure I do... for two "Wrenched"...
Professor Sakharov's latest research has proven that so-called zombies are just stalkers who haven't cured their hangover yet.

A dealer is walking quietly and peacefully toward Freedom's base with a sack of weed... Wham, into a "Springboard"!\n One guard at the gate says to the other:\n - What an asshole! Smoked it all on the way here again!

A knock at the shelter door.
 - Who is it?
 - It's me, the Bloodsucker!
 - And?
 - That's all...

A Pseudogiant is walking down the road. Suddenly a tushkano comes up to him and says:
 - Take your pants off!
 - Why? the Pseudogiant asks in surprise.
 - Need to. What, are you sorry or something? the tushkano replies.
 - Fine... the Pseudogiant grumbles.
 The tushkano inspects them carefully from every side and says:
 - Nah, these aren't mine. Mine had a little pocket...

A reporter in the sewers is interviewing a Bloodsucker:
 - And what do you feed on?
 - Pals, artifacts.
 - What kind?
 - Come on, I'll show you, PAL...
A young stalker runs up to a pseudodog and starts stroking its fluffy tail. A veteran shouts in horror:\n - What are you doing? It'll tear you apart!\n - No, man, they don't bite from this end.

- Warrant Officer, guess what's heavier: a kilo of cotton or a kilo of scrap iron?
 - A kilo of scrap iron, of course!
 - Wrong. They weigh the same.
 - Yeah? Let me crack you over the head first with a kilo of cotton, then with a kilo of scrap iron, and then you'll know which one's heavier.

Two stalkers are crawling across the perimeter. Suddenly one of them plants his hand in a pile of shit. The other starts laughing. The first one, clapping a filthy hand over his mouth:\n - Quiet, you'll alert the military!

A Freedomer is sitting there smoking a joint when a zombie walks up to him:
 - Buddy, let me take a drag.
 The guy calmly lets him, and the zombie shuffles off after taking a puff.
 Then he sits there wondering why the guy wasn't scared... So he rips off one arm and one leg. Walks up again:
 - Buddy, let me take a drag.
 - Here.
 The zombie shuffles off again and wonders why the guy still wasn't scared.
 He tears off his remaining leg and rips open his belly. Walks up to the Freedomer again.
 - Buddy, let me take a drag.
 - No-o-o-o, from the look of it, that's bad for you...

Duty's checkpoint in front of the Bar. From the Wild Territory side come a terrible roar, wild gunfire, whining pseudodogs, and an inhuman bellow:
 - F*ck!!! Holy sh*t!!! C*cks in your mouth!!! Stop the f*ck still!!! You f*ggot pricks!!! Bite the damn heel off already!!!
 Then silence. Around the corner comes Sakharov, politely adjusting his gas mask, and addresses everyone:
 - Sorry. I was upset...

At Garbage, in the hangar.
 A veteran stalker, looking dead serious, is dragging himself toward the toilet out in the yard, hauling an enormous heavy backpack with him. The backpack's stuffed full: brand-new guns still covered in grease, ammo sorted into boxes, a heap of artifacts each wrapped in its own little bag, his whole suit, everything.
 From inside the hangar comes a rookie stalker's voice:
 - Buddy, why are you dragging your backpack into the toilet?
 - I'm in that kind of mood. Feel like taking a shit on my whole life.

I'm walking up to the checkpoint by the bridge, looking for Kuznetsov, and from there I hear:\n - Hey, stalker! Come here, we'll talk! Got canned food, we'll eat! Got fire-water, we'll drink! Got a tambourine, then however, we'll sing! shouted Private Худайбердыев [TERM?]...\n UKRAINE IS AN INDEPENDENT DEMOCRATIC STATE, however...
Two rookie stalkers are walking through the Zone. One says to the other:\n - Listen, I heard some bandit yelling about olives. I absolutely love olives. Shall we go catch some?!

Some Duty members are sitting by the campfire. One says:
 - I'm giving us away...
 - Why, you traitor!
 shouts another one, extra vigilant, shooting the first in the head.

A Freedomer comes to see the Doctor.
 - Feeling like shit, Doc!
 The Doctor listens to him and says:
 - You ought to drink some herbs, my good man.
 - Maybe I should smoke some syringes while I'm at it?

A drunk stalker crawls into "Сто рентген [TERM?]" on all fours. He turns toward the left wall and starts crawling up it. Then he climbs across the ceiling, slides down the other wall to the counter, orders a bucket of vodka, downs it in one go, turns around, and crawls back out the same way.
 One stalker says to another:
 - DID YOU SEE THAT?!
 - Yeah. Didn't even have a chaser!

Some Duty men line the thugs up against a wall.
 The warrant officer commands: "Ready!"
 Commands: "Aim!"
 Suddenly one of the thugs sneezes loudly.
 The firing squad, in unison:
 - Bless you!
Voices in the Zone:\n - So what are we going to do with you?\n - Comrade Pseudogiant, excuse me, I didn't know the underbarrel grenade launcher wouldn't take you down with the first shot...

Military briefing before a raid into the Zone:\n - You have accidentally stepped into a "Whirligig" anomaly. Your actions?\n - Rise twenty meters into the air, then scatter in pieces over a wide area.

Two Bloodsuckers are sitting there with a bottle of vodka. A stalker walks by.
 - Hey, stalker, will you be our third?
 - I will!
 - Excellent. We've already drunk the other two.

- You hear about the incident at Duty's base?
 - So what happened to them?
 - A Controller got right into the radio room. Spent half an hour recruiting for "Грех [TERM?]."
 - What's the point?
 - Duty members are straightforward guys: if one of their own is recruiting, then it must be the right thing to do. They spent the whole day looking for this "Грех [TERM?]" so they could join it.

An old stalker and a young stalker are chatting by the campfire. The young one asks:
 - Listen, do you think a soul can catch cold?
 - Ah, youngsters these days are so ignorant... Just think about it: how can a "Soul" catch cold if it's an artifact?
 - Strange, strange... the young one replies. I heard this in Sidorovich's cellar: "My soul, put your slippers on, the concrete floor is cold, you'll catch cold..."

A Freedomer is standing on the bank of Припять [TERM?], smoking a joint and staring at the waves, muttering:
 - Fishy, fishy, give me a new house, a woman with everything in the right place, and more food! Come on, don't be stingy!
 One of the egghead ecologists walks by:
 - What are you standing here for? You know fish don't talk!..
 A huge gold fish sticks its head out of the water and says:
 - That's exactly what I've been telling this idiot for the last damn hour! Read too much Pushkin, you stoned pothead!
A stalker is walking through some ruins, sees a door with a skull and crossbones and red letters saying, "Don't go in, it'll kill you!"\n He went in.\n It killed him.

Major Kuznetsov and his greenhorns on the Cordon are doing a crossword:
 - Night duty starting with a P?
 - Patrol?
 - No, doesn't fit the letters...
 - Picket?
 - No, what are you talking about, that's not the official term!
 - Post?
 - Comrade Major, look on the last page!
 - Pajamas!
Two stalkers are drinking vodka. A third one walks up:\n - Oh-ho! Vodka! I'll be the third!\n - No, the fourth. We've already sent three on ahead.
The lack of vodka is vodka's only flaw.
A stalker tossed a sock at the ceiling and it stuck there. "A gravitational anomaly," the experienced stalker thought.

Two drunk stalkers are walking through the swamp (well, crawling, to be exact). Suddenly a Snork rushes past on all fours. One stalker says to the other:\n - L-l-listen, buddy, I don't get it! Look at that drunken bum. Did we drink brake fluid or something?

A veteran stalker is walking through the woods when out of nowhere a huge Chimera appears in front of him and says in a terrible voice:\n - Well, veteran, has the powder in your flask gotten damp yet?!\n Right then the veteran's powder got damp...

One day Swamp Doctor is walking through the Zone with his dog, when a stalker comes toward him:\n - Hey, Doctor! Why doesn't your dog bark? He lets strangers get awfully close.\n - Well, think it through yourself: if a pseudodog doesn't let strangers get close, how is it supposed to eat them?

A group of hard-case Freedomers is heading out of the Zone toward the Cordon, bristling with weapons, all in exoskeletons.
 Major Kuznetsov, after counting out the cash due at the usual rate, asks:
 - You boys heading into town?
 - Yeah...
 - Mind taking the mail with you?
 - What, you want us to seize the telegraph, the telephone, and the bridges too?!

A rookie stalker calls a friend. The friend answers in a languid voice:\n - I won't talk to you. I've fallen into depression...\n The rookie stalker thought: "I'll have to ask the veterans what kind of anomaly this depression is."

A Freedomer walks up to a Duty man:
 - Sir, you are a scoundrel! I demand satisfaction and challenge you to a duel! I grant you the choice of weapons: assault rifle or knife?
 - Knife.
 - Excellent. Then I choose the assault rifle!
- Pseudogiant, do you love nature?\n - Yea-a-a-a-a-a-a-ah!\n - Even after what it did to you?

On Tuesdays and Thursdays, toward evening, Sidorovich would hang out a sign reading "Break for German lesson" and lock the bunker. Late stalkers would kick the rusty door in annoyance while muffled phrases drifted through it: "Ja, ja! Das ist fantastisch!".
A soldier caught a stalker at the perimeter:\n - Now I'll show you how to sneak into the Zone!\n - Phew, thank God, because this is already the third time they've caught me!

Three Freedomers are sitting by the campfire... Nothing to do... bored to death... so they decide to play Russian roulette. The first pulls out a round, loads it, pulls the trigger. Bang. Drops. The second pulls out a round, loads it. Bang, drops. The third keeps watching, then pulls one out, loads it, fires. The last thought in his head:\n "Do they really play roulette with a Makarov?"

Relations between stalkers and the military.
 Checkpoint: "Halt, who goes there?"
 Stalker: "Friendlies, with a bottle!"
 Checkpoint: "Friendlies, move along! Bottle, halt!"
Cocktail: "Three Fleshes"!\n Recipe:\n A crate of vodka and three stalkers...

A stalker reached the Monolith.
 Monolith: "I will grant 3 of your wishes..."
 Stalker: "Well, fig me!.." (and figs, the exotic fruit, started raining down on him).
 Stalker: "Pancakes... what the horseradish..." (and pancakes started raining down on him, and horseradish, the seasoning, fell on him).
 Stalker: "THIS IS SHIT!"

A female Pseudogiant is chasing tushkanos around a clearing. She runs and runs, trying to catch them, but they keep getting away. She gets tired, goes berserk, and tears after them roaring: "Hold still, you tampons!"

Two stalkers:
 - Every day you look more and more like a drunk.
 - Why's that?
 - Because your hands shake whenever you lift a bucket of vodka to your mouth!

A Controller is walking through the Zone, leading three pseudofleshes behind him. The first runs along shouting:
 - B... B... Br... Bya... B...
 The second:
 - Boo... Brooo... Boo...
 The third keeps up and chimes in:
 - Byyy... Bryyy... Byii... By...
 The Controller sighs:
 - Could really use a broad...

Two Freedom guys are sitting there smoking with a sack of weed.
 - Heh-heh, l-l-listen, Колян [TERM?], heh-heh, what'll happen to me if I smoke the whole sack at once?
 - Well, you'll start bleeding from your nose, eyes, ears.
 - Damn, no kidding... why?
 - Because if you smoke the whole sack by yourself, you bastard, I'll smash your face in!

Two Jewish stalkers are walking along:
 - Мойша [TERM?], did you know Сёма [TERM?] is a fag!
 - What, he borrowed vodka and won't give it back?
 - No, in the good sense...
A stoned Freedomer is lying by the river:\n - Beaver, why are your teeth so white?!\n - You need to smoke less if you're talking to beavers!

Stalkers are sitting by the campfire bragging.
 Duty member: "This evening I saved some rookie out of an anomaly."
 Mercenary: "And at lunchtime I helped one rookie fight off some bandits."
 Freedomer: "Well, this morning I got one sad rookie high..."
- Hey! What was good while I was gone? \n - Everything was good while you were gone.

In the psych ward, former stalkers are watching the morning Zone news on TV. One of them suddenly slaps his knee: 
 - Good thing I'm in the loony bin! 
 Behind his back, the orderlies say to each other: 
 - Yeah... Looks like this one's cured.
Attention, sensation! A Freedomer raised by dogs has been found in the Zone! Interestingly, he's the only well-mannered Freedomer in the Zone.

A young Bloodsucker asks an old one: 
 - How do you hunt stalkers? 
 - Well, first I let him spot me, then I make a big circle around him, then a slightly smaller one, and only then do I run up and grab him. 
 - I get it. But why run circles when you can sneak up from behind and grab him right away? 
 - Sure, you can do it that way too, but then you'll have to eat him with all the shit still inside him.

A stalker wandered around Freedom's base and asked: 
 - Guys, how do you even entertain yourselves around here? 
 - We've got a cook. Every Sunday we put him in a dress and take him dancing with the whole base. 
 - Well, that's not for me, the stalker says. 
 - Our cook says the same thing, that's why we do it with the whole base.

A Duty member walks into the deep woods and sees a Controller with his face smashed in, sitting there crying. 
 - What's wrong with you? 
 - Stalkers keep coming through here, and every one of them wants to mess with me. There aren't any women in the Zone, so every last one wants to have his way with me. 
 - Then fight back, the Duty man says. 
 - How am I supposed to fight back when they're loaded with weapons and threatening me with grenades? 
 - Then yell for help. 
 - I do yell! But who's going to hear me? It's the middle of nowhere out here! 
 The Duty man stood there, looked left, looked right, scratched his stubble, and started pulling off his suit. Controller: 
 - E-e-eh... What are you doing? 
 - So you're saying it's the middle of nowhere out here?..

A stalker is walking through the Zone and sees a Bloodsucker. 
 - What are you snorting for? 
 The Bloodsucker says nothing. The stalker smacks him around and moves on. 
 Another stalker comes along and sees the Bloodsucker. 
 - What are you huffing for? 
 The Bloodsucker says nothing. The stalker smacks him around and moves on. 
 Yet another stalker comes along and sees the Bloodsucker. 
 - What are you sitting there for? 
 The Bloodsucker says nothing. The stalker smacks him around and moves on. 
 An annoyed voice from behind: 
 - What are you sitting for, what are you snorting for... I live here, damn it!

A veteran Duty man walks in on some bandits. 
 - Well, well! Look who's rolled up! Buddy, got a smoke? 
 - I already explained to you last time, in plain language, that I don't smoke. 
 - Sorry, buddy, didn't recognize you. My eye's swollen shut, and anyway I only just came around and got back on my feet after your last visit.
A Bloodsucker walks into the bar and asks for beer. The stunned Barkeep: \n - Ye-e-eah... Bloodsuckers don't come in here very often... \n - No wonder! Beer is ten bucks!
A Bloodsucker is walking through the desert, and coming toward him are Петька [TERM?] and Василий Иваныч [TERM?]... \n - Got anything fresher?

Two stalkers walk into the bar and see a notice: "Buying Bloodsucker heads. One head - 100$". 
They turn right around and head for the Wild Territory. They search until nightfall. Finally they spot a Bloodsucker standing there. They quickly drop it with two barrels, cut off its head, and camp nearby for the night... 
In the morning, one wakes up, climbs out of his sleeping bag, and sees ten Bloodsuckers standing all around their camp. Tentacles spread out, eyes gleaming, growling nasty. The stalker quickly shakes the next sleeping bag and wakes his friend up happy: 
 - Get up, pal - we're rich!
- What's the difference between Tushkanos in Africa and Tushkanos in the Zone? \n - If you meet Tushkanos in Africa, they run away from you. If you meet Tushkanos in the Zone, you run away from them.

Stalkers are sitting by the campfire swapping stories. One says: 
 - So I'm walking through Red Forest one time. Suddenly I see blind dogs surrounding me... 
 - And then what? 
 - I see things are bad. So I climb the nearest tree, quick as I can... 
 - And then? 
 - I'm sitting in the tree, and the dogs are pacing around below, snapping their teeth. Then suddenly the branch under me goes crack! 
 - And? 
 - What do you think? They damn well ate me!

The Bar. The door flies open and a Chimera walks in, holding a thousand-ruble note in its teeth. It goes up to the counter, puts the money down, and says: 
 - Vodka and a tomato! 
 The unflappable Barkeep pours vodka into a bowl and puts a tomato beside it. The Chimera laps up the vodka, eats the tomato, and leaves at its own pace. The Bar is frozen with shock. Suddenly a drunk stalker's voice comes from the corner: 
 - No, did you see that? Chasing vodka with tomatoes?

A Controller appeared near the bar. Got in everyone's way, grabbed people's brains now and then. One stalker was given a job to clear out the Controller. One day he was gone, two days, three. That's it, everyone thinks, the Controller zombified him. They were just about to send scouts when here comes that stalker, drunk off his ass. He goes into the bar and orders a crate of vodka and snacks. Leaves. Again he's gone one day, two days, three. On the fourth day he comes into the bar, swaying, and says: \n - That's it, Barkeep, the Controller won't be coming back. \n - You mean you drank with him and worked something out? - No, Barkeep, I was drinking with my friend Федька [TERM?]. I showed the Controller a photo of my mother-in-law. You should have seen him run when he saw that picture.

Marked One bursts into Sidorovich's cellar, pulls out a hundred bucks, and casually tosses it onto the table. \n - Give me meds for all of it! \n - No, Marked One, I am not tearing one little bandage into four pieces.

What is there to do here anyway? This is basically kindergarten, a nursery. Sure, if you don't hang around here a bit, the Zone will finish you in five minutes flat. A smart man gets his edges knocked off first, then climbs into the devil's own backyard. Besides, you can get hold of gear or a weapon here, no problem. Everyone does.

If I could just wait for these uniformed pretty boys to take a lunch break... No point sitting here; I need to get back to the mainland, and these bastards won't let us through. I'm not the only one itching for the exit, but so far we still can't slip past.

The boys in uniform needed something nearby. Nobody used to come here at all; everything was stripped clean before I ever showed up, even the cables were cut out. And now they found something, looks like... I couldn't even get close to the building. They spotted me right away and opened fire. At least I got out without another hole in my hide.
Back off. Trying to survive, that's all.

We're putting together a team of men who aren't afraid to go underground in the Zone. There's an old plant, and all kinds of service tunnels under it; they say there's plenty down there, always something to profit from. Interesting, too...
Still thinking... They say Strelok's main base used to be somewhere around here... Makes me curious. Maybe there are still traces from back then, if you look hard enough?
I need to step away for a bit... Damn it, I nearly got nailed an hour ago! I was here half a year back, quiet as a grave, and now there are shaved-heads in tracksuits under every damn bush! Unreal...

You're lucky, stalker, that the bandits didn't catch you warm: they've got a base right nearby. Ah, if it were up to me, I'd hit it hard... For now we're just watching. That's their support point; the main one is closer to the center of the Zone.
They say there used to be a serious laboratory here, classified. Ah... On the one hand, if we could find the entrance, there must be loot in there. On the other, it feels damn sketchy...

Need to look around first. You know the present-day Dark Valley had a research complex before the disaster, an underground one at that? I don't know what it was for, but I doubt it was anything good. People tell all kinds of stories about it, mostly bad ones. Scary ones, even.

What? What do you want to hear? I'll tell you this: the Duty grunts may be cops, sure, but they know how to keep order, I'll give them that. Still don't like them. Everywhere they act like they own the place: don't take that, don't carry this out of the Zone... On top of that, they've already decided what you can say on the mainland and what is forbidden. Word is they want to destroy the Zone bit by bit. Soldiers, even former ones, don't surprise me... dreamers. Worse thing is, they say they've got support from the very top.

We will maintain order! This territory is now controlled by Duty, and the law here is Duty's rules. We control the Zone's borders and cool off hot heads: lunatics come here by the trainload, romance lovers... then die in bunches on the very first day.

Nothing from me, but I advise you to look around. Have you been to our transit base? It isn't only ours, truth be told; you can meet all kinds there. Main thing is, the place is good, clean of filth, and all the news from the Zone flows through it. You can almost always hear something useful, or just chew the fat with people about life.

I want to rest. You should head to the camp too; you'll hear all the rumors there. Everyone on their first time in, rookies like you, usually stops by us to pick up some experience or rest a little. We've got soldiers posted nearby. Don't be too scared of them, but don't show yourself too much either; they might shoot, easy as breathing. Not far from the trader, the local middleman, there's a place where you can set up a quiet little camp.

You should drop by the Bar and talk to people. Right now it's safe, stalker, and we're trying to keep it that way. There's always a crowd here: some have settled in for good and don't go beyond the nearby area, others are preparing for the next raid. Shame Ирокез [TERM?] never came back. He liked setting first-timers like you on the right path.

Heard Duty announced recruitment into its proud ranks? I thought about it for a minute, then decided: why the hell would I need that, back on the belt every other day? Discipline, this and that... I already served my two years, and that was enough...

We're heading out on a raid soon. And if you haven't heard yet, I'm telling you: we've announced additional recruitment. If you've got more or less some experience, come by. You won't regret it. Ask for Petrenko at the base.

I ought to drop by the arena... You came to watch the fights too, maybe place a little bet?.. Well now! There's an arena around here somewhere, and you know nothing. So here's the deal: Duty members catch mutants, right? After the research, if the beasts are still alive, they release them into the arena. And those little darlings come in all kinds: sometimes you can die laughing, sometimes you can piss your pants.

We just supplied the arena with fresh meat. Entertainment for the people, and no shame in us earning a cut from the bets. Besides, it proves to the stalkers one more time that Duty doesn't just flap its tongue, it actually does the work.

Listen, have you been to "Росток [TERM?]"? It used to be quiet there, but now going alone is rough: a proper wild field, too many mutants breeding. Come with me, eh?.. Though... I don't know you, and you don't know me. You never know. That's it, man, sorry. We split.
Nothing much... We just came from "Росток [TERM?]". I don't advise you to poke your nose in there, especially alone. Want to live? Then don't go.

I was thinking of heading to Yantar, then changed my mind. The eggheads have a real outpost of science there in the Zone. They're digging around, who knows in what, and zombies wander around the complex. Idyllic, by God.

We just got back... Recently cleared the Yantar area... yeah, carved up plenty of those sons of bitches! There are research laboratories there, but zombies are always thick around them. We regularly have to run over and help the scientists.

Maybe I should head to the warehouses... You know, by the old warehouses? Freedom's got a base there. They're carefree, decent guys; sitting with them over a bottle of something good is pure pleasure. And Duty doesn't keep darting around like they do near the Bar.

We're starting up a job again. There's an old factory nearby, and we keep driving the bandits out of it, over and over, but every time they come crawling back! Hasn't even been a month since the last time, and there they are again. Be careful around there...
We're organizing a raid. Freedom dug in behind the old warehouses... those stoned screwups, damn them...
I'm leaving, and you should leave too, while it still isn't night! By day it's more or less tolerable here, but once the sun goes down...
Stalker, leave! We're about to pull the cordon back. For now we only hold it during the day, and at night we leave. It's too damn bad here after dark.
You see, at night one must take shelter here, preferably behind good doors. During the dark hours we are effectively under siege.
Well, pal, I can see you're not in the know. At night you should be sleeping somewhere far away from here. Around here at night even a grenade launcher doesn't always help!
You know what? Let the Duty members, Freedomers, and marauders decide whose chunk this is and what they'll do with it. I don't give a damn! To hell with all of it...
We're working here right now, stalker. There's already enough filth breeding around, and now these mercenary bastards and Freedom idiots are making life harder...
I must warn you that it is rather unsafe here even during the day. Shootouts happen from time to time; as far as I understand, there is some kind of confrontation between local bandit groups.

We've got plans... Listen, tell me this: by rights, this is our territory, yes? It's easier for us to hold back the filth coming from the center of the Zone, yes? Let the eggheads dig around, who's bothering them, but Duty and the marauders can get the hell out of here!

I'm heading to the stalkers... What, you don't know? They've worn a new path through here so they can sneak loot straight to the perimeter past the Duty patrols. From Yantar to here, then by the back ways to Agroprom, and that's it, you're there!

Nothing. I don't know a damn thing. Just scraping by, gathering enough for daily bread. Going deep into the Zone scares me, to hell with that. There's no work at home, my wife nags me... A man's got to live somehow, right? Here at Garbage, if you walk around for a day, you can gather a few small artifacts here and there. Enough to eat and drink, basically.
What are you doing here? Heading to Yantar, or trying to smuggle artifacts past the Bar on the quiet?

I will not conceal that your colleagues, the stalkers, occasionally offer us rather interesting anomalous formations, artifacts, as you call them. Due to their... let us say, uncertain control, these places are something like a busy smuggling trail.

Come on, man. We made this path so we don't have to run into the Duty officials if we want to make a trip to the mainland! Sure, they roam around here plenty, but while they still don't know a damn thing, at least you can get around them.

I just came from Yantar. Thought I'd sell the scientists a few things, like always, but they've locked themselves in their bunkers and barely come outside even during the day now, except for the next chopper. So if you're headed there, turn yourself around. You're out of luck...
Some kind of research is being conducted in the Yantar area, but lately the zombies have been pressing in. That's why we're guarding the approaches.

I do not even know what to tell you. I had wanted to return to our complex, Yantar, I believe you know it. And now here we are: they say that if it was merely dangerous before, now getting through has become completely impossible. Perhaps they are exaggerating?

I was at Yantar, and science has withered badly there: flinching, hiding... They only stay alive thanks to what gets flown in by chopper. On the other hand, there really are plenty of zombies around there. Live among them for a week and you'll start flinching too. Not much of a place. Not very pleasant.
To hell with hanging around zombies! They've multiplied all over that damned complex, pushing in and pushing in. And there are more of them every day.
We're trying to stop the zombies from advancing out of the laboratory complex. Honestly, I wouldn't mind if you joined in. We're short on people...
You know, we collect various data ourselves... I personally study the main routes by which quasi-liv... that is, zombies, in your terminology, spread. Would you share your observations?

That's about it... Some people settle in at Garbage and pick up small stuff, some dig deeper into the Zone. The chunks out there are fatter, sure, but not everyone will stick his nose in. Rookies with no experience have no business there, and if they don't have decent gear either, that's lights out. No chance.
Have you shot at the zombies crawling out of the laboratory building yet? Just look at them: afraid of nothing, feel no pain. Brainless beasts, in a word!
I'm heading to the laboratory to apply for a job! Relax, man, kidding... If you wander in there, that's it, count yourself gone. Best case, you become a zombie.
I'm warning you: don't go into the laboratory. No, if you really want to, go ahead, I won't hold you by force. Just nobody comes back alive from there.

You see... I would like to warn you. You should not go near the laboratory. No one should, actually: it involves drastic changes to the psyche and metabolism, and I am afraid they are completely irreversible.

There! Now I'll enlight... educate... save you, lost sheep, in short. So here it is, man: don't aim your skis at the laboratory complex. It's bad there, very bad. People disappear, and then sometimes they come to you at night. Only these aren't vampire fairy tales, but the plain truth about zombies. That's how it is.

Have you seen our smart ones, the cream of the United Nations? They've gotten nervous as hell lately. For them to take two steps from the base, they call in a whole regiment for protection, with APCs and choppers.
What's it to you? Stop pestering people with stupid questions and people won't hassle you either.

You understand, we usually do not leave the bunker without appropriate escort: environmental conditions do not permit it. I am something of an exception. I do not much care for large expeditions, all that bustle, the military... However, I am afraid I cannot tell you anything useful for now.

I get a kick out of our cheerful science! It blows my mind, man!.. Usually it doesn't poke its nose out of the bunker. But when it does, like it did recently, then things get wild. Good Lord! Soldiers everywhere, choppers, armored vehicles! It would be even better if they dragged a battleship in here, or an armored train at least...

Third day I've been hanging around here, watching. There's a laboratory here, right? Nobody has gone into it since the beginning, and the Duty boys only cleared zombies above ground, but now they've stopped even that, right? But the scientists have started going in there. Means they found some way to protect themselves, hell knows from what... So I'm thinking I'll find out. There must be loot in that place you could shovel up.

In the last few days, beasts have been pouring out of the Dark Valley in whole packs. No idea what's driving them out of there, but they're charging like it's a fire. And the eastern sector has mutants breeding like bedbugs; all we can do is keep shooting them. That's the news.

For now I'm watching you... What are you looking for here? Planning to head into the laboratory too? Heard a little fairy tale about miracle devices and piles of artifacts? M-mother... when will you idiots finally die out?

I work here, young man. I simply work here, fulfilling my duty to science and humanity. And if you want to ask about the abandoned laboratory, whose secrets every stalker I meet suspects me of knowing, my answer is: I do not know and do not want to know! This subject does not interest me and never has, is that clear?!

Maybe the brainiacs really did come up with some device, you never know. It is interesting, what's hidden in that damned laboratory. Even if there isn't any proper loot, it is still interesting. If only I could get in...

I'm thinking: maybe I should join Freedom? Their base is right there, in the former army warehouses... Why not? The people seem competent, and if they're a bit careless, that's even good. No one needs to crush me with discipline; I know what to do, this isn't my first month here...

We're getting ready to kick somebody's ass... You don't sympathize with Freedom by any chance, do you? Fine, fine, don't take offense. I think we'll soon pry them out of those warehouses. No organization, no discipline... A herd. A pack at best. And now they got it into their heads to twitch against us, NATO-fed bastards...

I want to talk to someone. I meet with one Duty man here when there's a truce. We chat. The man is smart, generally, but narrow-minded as a tank. You see, our philosophy is completely different: the Zone can only be tamed, assimilated, not destroyed. For that, we need open access to all information; sooner or later somebody will come up with something useful. And they go: "close it," "forbid it," "do not disclose"... Idiots, by God, idiots. On the other hand, it makes sense: when the whole state machine is behind you, you're not really supposed to think too much.
There's a little farmstead here. It used to be quiet, but now some kind of filth has bred there. Better to take the long way around, so that's what I'm doing... Look, I warned you.
Stalker, I want to warn you right away: Bloodsuckers have nested in the village for a long time now, maybe something even worse too, so be careful.

What is there to do here... There haven't been artifacts here for a long time, and Bloodsuckers used to live in those houses over there. We shot them out as best we could. Now I went that way and I can't make myself reach the place. Intuition, or whatever, but I can't go there, that's all. No way. Death is there...

I'm getting the hell out of here! Did four weeks on Барьер [TERM?], and that's it, I've had enough! Some kind of filth has been coming from the Brain Scorcher side around the clock, and every day it pushes harder...

A lot of people have become "garbage pickers" now. Used to be nobody even looked in those heaps; the radiation would hard-boil your balls in half an hour. But now, with loot getting scarce, they wrap themselves in protection, take some chems, and go digging anyway. You can find all kinds of things there, though mostly small stuff.
If you're heading to our defense line, I respect you. If not, get out of sight! We need real men, not screwups. We've got enough of our own...

You heading to the defense line, man? That's good then. At least one more normal person shows up. We set the whole thing up and held it pretty well... but then the beasts came po-o-ouring in!.. It's rough now, so every gun helps.
What, you haven't heard? The path to the center of the Zone is open! Maybe it opened on its own, maybe someone helped it along, and honest thanks to him. Now it's time to rake in the prime loot!

You should wait, stalker. Don't run after everyone else. Tell me this: do you know what's there? Right, you don't. You don't know whether there's anything to profit from there or not. You don't know what traps are there either. So why the hell are you charging in like a herd of moose?!
The path to the center is open, got it?! Straight road to the center now! Everyone is about to pile in there. Main thing is to be among the first and squeeze out the best stuff for yourself!

Move it, you'll see everything yourself! Don't you want to see for yourself what's going on farther in? Then you're really... touched. There must be so much there! Say, have you heard about Исполнитель желаний [TERM?]?
I'm not doing anything... Like children, by God! "Исполнитель желаний [TERM?]," "Klondike of artifacts"... It's laughable just listening to it!
I want to know myself what's beyond the horizon, man! What if we can explain all this shit they call the Zone in one shot?
See the apartment blocks? That's Припять [TERM?] already. Right behind it is the station. Ah, if only I could get there... except nobody knows what's wandering among those buildings now.

I'll explain. There is Припять [TERM?]. Past Припять [TERM?] is the CNPP. What is going on there now, and what lives there, nobody knows. This is the place for us. There will be things to sort out, same as always.

Not a clue! I'm just heading to the Bar to talk to people. What, you've never been there? You're something else! Here's the deal: from where you're standing, go straight north. You won't make it two steps before there's the Bar. It's useful to stop in there in general, and for rookies like you it's just what the doctor ordered: experienced men hang around there sometimes, they'll tell you what's what. Only thing is, our Duty vigilantes will meet you on the way... Well, whether they like the look of you or not, that's how the cards fall.
Припять [TERM?]! Pinch me! Finally! The station is almost within arm's reach from here. Hell knows who you might run into here now, though, what kind of beast has its teeth sharpened for you...
Friend, listen... Do you hear a voice in your head too, or have I just lost my mind? It keeps droning on and on...

Another one... All right. I will explain for the umpteenth time what kind of telepathic influence people are subjected to here. Voices inaudible from outside constantly call them to the center of the Zone and promise to fulfill any wish of whoever manages to touch... what was it... ah, yes, some kind of monolith.
You hear the voice? Funny, like a player in your head! Shame there's only one track: come to me, it says, and great happiness will be yours. Two hours now, same thing over and over.
Like you haven't guessed? You're breaking through to the station yourself! And so are we, what other news do you need?! If only we could clear those possessed idiots off the road...
The station grounds are very close! We still can't overcome the Monolith members' resistance!..
There it is, the CNPP, within arm's reach. Almost nothing left, and these stubborn bastards won't let us through! Doesn't matter, we'll straighten them out now!

Listen, friend, do you know anything about Agroprom? I wanted to go there, but people laughed me down: they say there's not a damn thing left there, it was picked clean back when only anomalies were left. Do you know if they're telling the truth, or just having a laugh? I'm new around here, I believe it when locals talk... Maybe they got together and pulled one over on me? Give those bastards half a reason and they'll laugh themselves sick!
Stop! Make haste, but don't rush too much. Monolith members are all over here; they know the city inside and out, planted ambushes everywhere. Need to be careful.

Warning: most of the city is currently controlled by the Monolith faction. There are many ambushes; they have studied this place like the backs of their hands. Stay alert and check your weapon one extra time.
Ah, what I'd gladly do is carpet-bomb this little city. Bastards... they must be dug in everywhere except the sandboxes and flowerpots. How many people have already died because of these assholes!

Listen, who the hell knows about this Исполнитель [TERM?]... Maybe it isn't eternal, so there won't be enough for everyone? And what then, get down on all fours? You, kid, if we make it, you'll be behind me. I promise you that...

Duty must break through to the center, if not first, then among the first! Just imagine, stalker, some psycho, and almost all of you here are one way or another schizophrenics, paranoids, and morons, reaches some piece of crap that really can grant any wish. Huh? And then what?!

News of the century: everyone who can walk on his own and hold a weapon is here now! This is... anything you wish could come true here! Just grasp the subject, friend, huh? Anything you wish! And then someone goes and says: "I want the Zone to vanish, now and ever, and unto ages of ages." Huh? Appreciate the scale of the tragedy? We'd all be left with nothing then. Every last one of us!

New? What the hell do we need new for when you can shovel the old shit forever and still not clear it out? The bandits have gotten completely bold, the bastards! When it comes to darting between anomalies for artifacts, that's us, but when it comes to taking someone else's loot... Ugh, scum! Watch you don't wander into Garbage. That's their favorite place.

I'm stuck here, stuck, you understand? I want to go home, and they won't let us through. That's all the news... The people who go deep into the Zone, the hardened ones, they do tell all kinds of stories. And sometimes they bring back loot that makes you jealous. They say there are places out there like gold fields: take all you want. Not everyone reaches them, though, and even fewer come back with a profit.

The soldiers are up to something. Searching, digging... Used to be choppers only flew to Yantar, guarding scientists, hauling supplies and equipment. Now it's a real invasion. They've started running sorties, driving around in APCs. They've already been spotted at Agroprom, and in Dark Valley too. No idea.

They say someone tried to get through the Scorcher again. Gone for good, obviously, man's finished... You haven't heard of the Brain Scorcher? Come on, man! It's the biggest bogeyman in the whole area! Only problem is, the horrors people tell about it are mostly true.

Besides the bandits, soldiers have started roaming around here too. Used to be you'd see a helicopter once in a hundred years, and now they've spread across almost the whole Zone. Lost something, maybe?

I want to warn you, stalker. The military has gone half-mad lately. They used to quietly guard the scientists, but now it's like someone twisted their tail: running around, rushing everywhere... They're not exactly peaceful. They've fired on your kind more than once, and we've had two wounded this week.

Выдра [TERM?] got killed at the Scorcher a week ago. If he had been a rookie, some headless greenhorn, sure, I'd understand. But he'd been stalking for years. They say he was even in Strelok's group. And there you have it...

Tell me this, stalker: why do you loners sometimes blow your roofs off? Not enough proper discipline, maybe? One of you decided to go past the Scorcher last week. Experienced man, they say... There are easier ways to lose your brains: barrel in your mouth and pull the trigger!

Those bastards... mercenaries. They've been drinking our blood for a long time, and now they've switched to the eggheads too. What the hell do they need them for? Ransom? Or so they'll share their Nobel prizes?

The mercenaries have gotten very active in recent weeks. We also call them marauders. That's another kind of filth here, if you haven't heard. Like bandits, only experts in military matters. They always preferred attacking stalkers, and lately for some reason they've become more interested in scientists. Either way, keep your eyes open. It costs them nothing to put a hole in your head too.
I heard in passing recently that the eggheads supposedly dug up something incredible. And now everyone is on edge at once: soldiers, mercenaries, and Duty members are not exactly lagging behind...

If you're asking about the same thing all of you have been pestering me with for the last two days, my answer is: go to hell! If I knew, and if they hadn't forbidden it, I'd tell you. But I don't know, I don't know a damn thing! What did those scientists find, where did our boss go? What's that got to do with me?! He's on leave, and he doesn't owe us a report.

Ha! Everyone here is on edge! Конфедерат [TERM?] came back from a raid with his group, and they brought artifacts, mother of God! Конфедерат [TERM?], of course, keeps quiet and won't spill, same as always, and his boys keep joking that they went to the Юконские прииски [TERM?] and panned a little... Bastards!

All kinds of filth have been pouring out from the Scorcher for several days now. People built a defense line near the old warehouses; for now, seems like they're holding. If it doesn't let up, we'll have to go reinforce them. What else can we do...
Heard people saying the Zone is growing? I think it's true. I saw some hideous beasts almost right at the perimeter, things you used to meet only closer to the center. Bad business, huh?

Maybe they're not lying about Bloodsuckers near the Zone border. But what does that have to do with the Zone getting bigger? A pack wandered in... we missed it, fine. We sometimes work for several days straight! And we do the job properly. Nobody beyond the perimeter is shouting yet: "Help, mutants are surrounding us!"
