It's all kind of grim, that's what. The other day Выдра [TERM?] vanished for no reason, I still can't believe it... You probably never even heard of Выдра [TERM?], have you? He was practically a professor, here since the first year, knew the whole Zone like the back of his hand... and still, look at that. There are experienced stalkers, sure, but the Zone takes them anyway. Some head back to the mainland by choice, too.

Do you think Barkeep knows the details about what the scientists dug up? Listen, maybe he's in with science altogether? Lately he's been looking for more experienced stalkers and giving them strange jobs. Explains nothing, just says go here or there... and the places are dead, never smelled of good loot. So what is he looking for?
Something strange is going on. Zombies that used to be afraid to come into the light have spread all over the area. What the hell is that about?

The zombies have become more aggressive and mobile for some reason. We used to clear them out inside the complex from time to time, but now we only hold the approaches. But I didn't tell you any of that, got it?
Be careful, because these walking corpses have flooded the whole area. They are extremely aggressive. We are constantly on defense, night and day. So it is rather dangerous here, young man.

Ah, the eggheads probably cooked up something wrong in their laboratory. Used to be you could barely find a zombie anywhere, and now they're pouring out of the complex. Main thing is, they wander around like they lost something and are looking for it.
There's a rumor going around that science actually talked one of ours into going down into the laboratory. Gave him some kind of... protective contraption... Nothing else, no details.
People say all kinds of things. They even say some maniac turned up, made a deal with the scientists, and crawled into the laboratory after something for them. What a stupid asshole!
Ah, here's the main joke of the week! The eggheads signed up one of yours for a fat fee, and he went into the laboratory with some device of theirs. A new sacrifice to science, damn it!..
I think something is brewing again. The eggheads dug up something that put everyone on edge. And along the way, life is going to get worse for you and me.
Who the hell knows. Only they're running us twice as hard as before: cordon here, sweep there... There are a whole order of magnitude more soldiers in the Zone now too.

It's grim, that's what I'll say. There have always been plenty of people on the outskirts, half of them never go deep into the Zone at all, and there aren't enough artifacts for everyone. Even the laboratories and factories have had everything that could be broken out already stripped and hauled away. Soon there'll be nothing to do here at all except open a restaurant... with a view and exotic cuisine.

I do not even know what to tell you... Something is always happening here, but that is normal. On the mainland, of course, they do not see it that way, but you and I are, after all, currently in an anomalous zone.

The eggheads are sniffing something out. Hell knows what secret they've already dug up, but they're twisting the soldiers' tails something fierce! Those men are already combing Agroprom and Dark Valley, searching and searching...

More filth comes pouring from behind the Scorcher every day, and Duty members and Freedomers have decided to start snapping at each other again. So they wipe each other out, and then what? How long can we hold Барьер [TERM?] alone?
Mutants are trying harder and harder to slip past the defense line. Lately it's been rough. If certain people would cut out their screwing around, we'd handle them easily!..

Duty keeps shouting about its heroic deeds at the defense line. Who around here would believe them... We started this thing, we keep dragging it along, and plenty of loners like you stayed voluntarily too. And those... to hell with them, bastards!

I heard Duty has gotten real full of itself. Supposedly "we are volunteers who want to achieve safety and order in the Zone"... yeah, volunteers! Some sponsor supplied those volunteers with gear and guns that would make you sick with envy. These ones sold out too.
What rumors are going around? I'll tell you without any rumors: soon there will be more order. We'll clean the Zone of beasts, and of certain bastards too...
Have you heard what the Duty boys are bragging they'll do to us? "Grind us to dust" is the mild version. This worries me... Especially since they say they've re-equipped first-class.

What news? We sit here, beasts come at us, we shoot them, and then they come again, only harder. True, military choppers passed through a few times... and it looked like they even flew past the Scorcher.

What could be new here... same old, same old. Mutants, Freedom... though now military helicopters have gotten into the habit of flying around. If only they'd fire at the beasts once. No, not a damn thing...

New... People say all kinds of things! Believe everything and you'd run out of belief. They say some veteran stalker, let me through or I'll break through, came from the inner regions, brought something never seen before, made a heap of money... I could invent a hundred stories like that for you myself!

I saw military choppers. Imagine it: they passed over us and went straight past the Brain Scorcher! So I thought, here they come raining down. Not a damn thing. They kept going, circled around there, then came back the same way. Nice move, huh?
Ah, now that there's a passage to the center... Just imagine how much wealth must be lying underfoot there!
Now that it is possible to pass to the center of the Zone relatively calmly, stalkers have flown there like flies to honey. Ye-e-eah... greed really is not humanity's finest quality.

Now this is life coming our way! By the time all those people from the Bar, especially from the perimeter, pull up, all the cream is ours! We'll gather the tastiest stuff, and let the others stock up on scraps.
They say it really exists, Исполнитель желаний [TERM?]. Someone has already managed to reach it and make a wish for everything he could...
Listen, pal, I know anything can happen in the Zone. Seen it myself. But these stories about a wish instantly granting your own hotel in the Caribbean are complete bullshit and nothing else.

What, you don't know?! Two of ours, the ones who rushed to the center before anyone else, actually broke through to Исполнитель [TERM?] and made their wish! They dreamed all their lives about islands in a warm sea, so now they're sunning themselves somewhere in the tropics, no doubt.
Миклуха [TERM?] turned up, the main Freedomer. Everyone thought he'd gone back to the mainland, but in reality he somehow managed to slip past the Scorcher with a small group. That's a real man!

One of Freedom's detachments was found in a previously inaccessible area. How did they manage to get through unharmed, then survive here for several weeks? It just doesn't fit in my head. Looks like we underestimated these boys.
Imagine that, our Миклуха [TERM?] turned up! Alive, and the boys with him survived too. They slipped past the brain-scrubber, then spent a month stuck here, unable to get out.

Something murky is brewing here. Rumor says the Duty commander, the big boss, didn't just leave the Zone on some business, but went to negotiate with Freedom. Then they should make peace openly, instead of breeding all this bullshit! This whole business smells bad, that's how it is. On the other hand, veterans say they once started fighting for a stupid reason too. Let's hope those office bastards from the mainland don't drag us all into some new meat grinder...
You hear?! The soldiers are rushing in to grab the ready-made prize, they'll be here any minute! They found out the Scorcher is done for and want to scoop up all the loot at once!..
Information just came through: a large-scale army landing at the station is planned. My advice to you: if you want to snatch even a little loot, hurry.

This is complete lawlessness! The corridor only just opened, they started clearing the station of all kinds of shit, and here you go, the soldiers are coming for everything ready-made! Damn it all, no shame and no conscience...
Here's what I don't understand: how did those Freedomers manage to hold out for a month? Do they have invisibility caps or something? It's a complete nightmare out here!
You understand what's going on here? No, really, I respect Миклуха [TERM?] even more now! Imagine that: lasting more than four weeks in this shit, and not losing a single man!..
Some people here have lost their minds. They always seemed like strong men, too, and now they're drooling like complete idiots. Scary.

As one approaches the station, the unknown telepathic influence intensifies. Some have begun losing their minds, others are suffering disorders and visions. Perhaps now is the right time to fall back a little...

Listen, people here are really losing their roofs. Somebody sees a little stone and suddenly starts yelling that it's his artifact, that he spotted it first and everyone else can go south. Another man sees Джа [TERM?] appear on a cloud, arm in arm with squadmates who vanished hell knows how many years ago... No, something seriously unhealthy is happening here.

Listen to this joke about a Controller. An old Controller is teaching a young one: "See that little soldier standing at his post way over there? Belt tight, uniform new, boots shiny: that's called a 'rookie.' You can take control of him from right here, but there's no point: he shoots badly, fears everything, no meat on him, just skin and bones. And over there, see that fighter smoking? Uniform worn, boots scuffed: that's called a 'scoop.' So-so prey, hard to take under control, shoots well, always alert, and plenty of meat, but stringy. Best to take him at night, when he dozes off. And way over the-e-ere, see that soldier sleeping under the bush, no belt, barefoot? That's an 'old-timer.' Lots of meat, fatty, but hell if you can get near him; he doesn't sleep at night and can crush you with his bare hands. You take him when he goes to the settlement for moonshine or drags himself into the Zone for loot. And over there, see that soldier walking, belt tight, boots shiny, but Zone forbid you confuse him with a rookie. I took one like that by mistake once, spent two weeks scrubbing my lair with a toothbrush, barely escaped. He's called 'Comrade Sergeant Major'."

Listen to this new joke, they told it in the bar. A stalker comes to Sidorovich one day. And Sidorovich says to him:
- Listen, stalker. I got a spacesuit from some secret Kyiv research institute, supposedly with one hundred percent protection from radiation. Only nobody has tested it yet. Go into the Zone wearing it, see what's what. Then I'll give you a discount on it, if you survive... The stalker agreed. Went into the Zone, and one day passes, then two, then three... On the fourth day he comes back, mean as a Snork, and slams the spacesuit onto Sidorovich's table without a word. Sidorovich is stunned:
- What is it, stalker? Doesn't protect you? 
- It protects me! 
- Not one hundred percent? 
- One hundred percent!
- Then what are you unhappy about? 
- The whole Zone treats radiation with vodka from morning till night, and I ALONE, LIKE AN IDIOT, AM SOBER FOR THE FOURTH DAY!!!

I saw Monolith members clearing the territory near the Scorcher the other day, about thirty men, killing anything that moved: Fleshes, Boars, even "trampling" dogs... So I, well... got out of there fast, just in case. I don't feel like catching lead in the ass.

Want to hear a joke? A "Нива [TERM?]" is tearing down the road toward the pre-Zone area, doing about 120 kilometers an hour. A traffic cop is blowing his whistle like a songbird, the "Нива [TERM?]" brakes, a whole squad of Duty boys climbs out, beats the soldier's ass, shoves the whistle up his ass, and races on. The traffic cop grabs his radio:
- Seven two-seven three!
- Seven two!
- Сеня [TERM?]! Some bastard is flying your way and won't react to the whistle! So stop him with the baton, the baton! Do you copy, seven-two?..

Man, have you ever heard of Фантом [TERM?], no? I'll tell you, you won't believe it. They say there was once a stalker in the Zone, don't know his name, everyone called him Фантом [TERM?]. He was experienced, and a good man too, understood people and they understood him. People said he studied psychology at an institute. Doesn't matter, though, getting to the main thing: he was slippery, could slip anywhere, checkpoints and anomalies weren't obstacles to him. He went deep into the Zone, then one day vanished, most likely got caught in an emission or something. Anyway, he became something like a poltergeist. For ten years now he's been walking the Zone, looking for other stalkers. When he finds you, he looks into your soul. If you're a good person, he'll help somehow: lead you to shelter, help you fight off mutants. But if you've got more sins than you can count, buddy, hide yourself, because he'll tear you to pieces. There's your bedtime story...

Marked One, where did you get a gun like that? Bought it from Sidorovich, I bet? Yeah, his guns are nice, but they cost way too much. The bastard probably buys them from the army for pennies and sells them to us at triple price! No thanks, buddy, I wasn't born stupid. I'd rather look for a gun at Garbage. Sure, it might glow so hard you start glowing too, but at least it's free.

Listen, stalker, somebody told me a good one the other day. Here it is. Duty members and Freedom stalkers are having it out. So the Freedom commander stands up and shouts:
- Forward, for Freedom! - and everyone charges, you understand. Duty stalkers on the other side of the front:
- That's it, we're screwed, time to run.
And an old, experienced Duty man says:
- Wait before you run, - pulls out a machine gun: tra-ta-ta-ta. The Freedomers drop. Five minutes pass, a Freedomer stands up again:
- For the Zone! Hurra-a-a-a-a-ah! - and they all charge again. Duty:
- Now we're definitely screwed, time to run.
The old Duty man:
- Wait! - grabs a Kalash and does his "tra-ta-ta-ta" again. So the Freedomers drop again. Some more time passes. The Freedom commander stands up again:
- For our boys, for Freedom in the Zone, forward! - and they charge again. Duty's young ones panic again. The old Duty man does his thing again. Freedom has to lie down again. Five minutes later the Freedom commander stands up. Face in shit, covered in shit, and roars: 
- Fuck, I don't get it!
The old Duty man, flicking his cigarette aside: 
- Now it's time to run.

Heard about the mercenaries? No? Then listen carefully and take notes: they're killers, buddy. Serious, desperate men too. They'll get anyone you want, alive or dead. Remember Сыч [TERM?], who got whacked recently? Precise shot to the heart and head, body burned, no traces at the scene. Their work, believe me. Those bastards have no conscience. Killing a man is no different to them than crushing a cockroach; they only respect coin. Rumor says they have a base in Мёртвый город [TERM?], but nobody has ever come back alive from there. They don't like strangers. And do you know how they train? They catch some bandit or stalker, bring him to their place, give him a Kalash with two magazines, and let him go in any direction. If the man isn't dead after 12 hours, he can keep living. That's their idea of fun, buddy. In all that time only Миша Косой [TERM?] managed to stay alive, and only because he served ten years in special forces. So, Marked One, if you see guys in blue suits, put your legs to work and run far away. Though if they've already set their eye on you, you won't get away anyway. They'll dig you out from underground.

They say there's one group of people... Sometimes they're called shamans, sometimes druids. They say they don't carry any electronic equipment at all, closer to nature and all that... Well, they say the Zone reacts more strongly to electronics, to all kinds of electromagnetic emissions... and sets monsters on you too. The way they see it, the Zone is a living organism. You can't go poking into it with devices, they say; a proper person can manage without any extra attachments. Though the bastards do carry assault rifles... Why not try teeth and claws, then... On the other hand, it seems not that many of them die in the Zone. No more often than us, anyway... So maybe they figured something out right... They mostly come into the Zone from the Беларусь [TERM?] side, though... Would be nice to get a look at them... And check how many artifacts they have in their pockets while we're at it...

Listen, Barkeep told me Bloodsuckers attacked the village near the Army Warehouses yesterday. Nobody got out alive, they didn't even manage to send a proper signal. Duty members went to check the next day. They say it was a slaughterhouse in there, boys cut up like pigs, scraps lying everywhere... horror, basically.

Got a cigarette? You don't smoke? Clear enough... Right thing to do, by the way. Good for the body, and keeps your lungs ready for running from dogs, heh-heh... Anyway, you know, some skilled mercenaries, damned snipers, used to drop stalkers in the dark by an interesting principle. When stalkers gathered in a group and relaxed with nicotine, the mercenary would spot the flare of the match and the cigarette glow when the first man lit up. Then, when the second stalker lit his cigarette, the match flame moved, the second smoke lit, and the sniper led the transfer of fire. The third lit up and immediately got a bullet in the head... That's why stalkers now have a rule: "Three do not light from one match." So jokes are jokes, but a man's life can depend on little things like that.

I ran into some dazed man here. He had an army suit, a special-forces rifle, and behaved like a pure zombie: one leg half dragging, movements unclear, body barely obeying him... I almost shot him at first. What stopped me was that he kept a tight grip on his rifle. I've seen plenty of zombies in my time, but never one like that. He was trying to tell me something too, only I barely made anything out... Only understood "Controller" and "help." I was just about to help the kid when I heard a chopper, got scared, and ran. Dove into a little grove. Seven soldiers appeared, then the helicopter. Three kept the kid in their sights, and the others rushed him and threw him to the ground. Imagine that, their own man, face in the mud! They were shouting something about some helmet. Then they dragged him under the arms into the helicopter... Something strange here... That kid, his words about the Controller... the soldiers... Did he really manage to get away from a Controller...

I recently managed to sit by the campfire with a mercenary. Turned out he wasn't a bad guy. He killed stalkers, as you understand, only on orders from his bosses, never just because. In general, mercenaries treat our stalker kind pretty well, especially experienced men and veterans. They respect us, though they consider only themselves unsurpassed masters. And that mercenary told me the story of his life. For his service, he has an Order of Courage and a Medal for Bravery. He studied at a sniper training center. Everything there was serious: weapon checks every time, and so on. But he said this: real sniper skills can only be acquired in war, in hot zones. Sometimes a man would be trained long and thoroughly, but once it came time for the exam, usually held in Чечня [TERM?], no, he couldn't do it, and that was that. In school you can prepare, shoot targets, hit them confidently, but at the most important moment something can switch on inside a person, and he simply can't do it. That's how it is. So you, Marked One, be more confident in the Zone. If something threatens your life, be confident. Psychology.

I'll tell you this: I got into a terrible scrape and barely crawled out. I was walking along, and suddenly bang! Everything around me went dark, horrible sound, like something was eating you, I almost had all my veins opened. Somehow I dumped some of my junk and crawled out. Luck actually helped: my sack was worn out, the strap snapped under the strain and pulled the fabric, opening a hole. Something fell out, and that's how I was saved. Fate! Good thing I had bandages with me. Took me a week to recover after that blood loss. That's some nasty shit. God forbid you get caught in it!

Have you heard about the tricky anomaly? Let me tell you, it's a real piece of filth! You can barely see it, like some black puddle, but if you fall in, you'll know right away. And Zone help you if you hesitate: shake something out of your backpack fast, and don't even think about what you need and what you don't. Anything, as long as you get out, or you won't have time. It'll eat you alive, I'm telling you straight, saw it myself. Thank God it didn't happen to me, but the boys explained afterward what that shit was.

Heard how one stalker got into "Проглотка [TERM?]"? Well, stalkers also call it "Жадинка [TERM?]," different people, different names... What, you've never heard of one at all? Then listen: there is such an anomaly, not common, but if you meet it, God help you if you step in... So this stalker was coming from somewhere loaded with good loot. And he was an experienced man, too, but somehow it happened... fatigue probably got him. I don't know what he went through in there; you can't stay in it for more than a minute, it'll eat you like nothing. But for him that minute must have felt like eternity. Crawled out barely alive, bleeding all over, suit in shreds, loot gone... Lucky he lived, and the boys nearby supplied him with bandages. He could have kept his loot, too. Why did it happen? Greed. He carried a lot and didn't want to throw anything away, so he hesitated, thinking. In a business like that, you can't think long. So my advice to you, God forbid, of course: if you get caught, throw whatever comes to hand and run for it.

Do you already know anything about anomalies? "Vortex," "Springboard," that sort... heard of them? Well, I'll tell you: all that is nothing compared to "Чёртова глотка [TERM?]," or "Жадинка [TERM?]" - they're the same thing. Why? Because if you fall into another anomaly, everything is clear: either you get out or you don't... If you get out, you're alive and your loot is with you, lucky, in short. Knock on wood... But if you land in this one, there's only one way out: throw something to it so it calms down, and crawl out at once. And that's the whole nasty part. Any stalker will hold out until the last second rather than part with his loot. But this is exactly the kind of case where you can't hold out under any circumstances. Hold out too long, and you'll be stretched out dead... Nice way I said that. But that's how it is. Remember it, it'll come in handy.
I sometimes ask myself: why the hell do we keep running around like this? To earn money? But what the hell do we need money for if all we do is keep running around?..
They showed me a new cocktail in the Bar, called "Witch's Jelly." It's the kind of thing, buddy, that's dangerous to take on an empty stomach: one serving and your arms and legs stop working...
I sometimes ask myself: why the hell do we keep running around like this? To earn money? But what the hell do we need money for if all we do is keep running around?..

One rookie was always afraid to stick his nose past the Cordon. Garbage? Dark Valley? No way, are you kidding, monsters and bandits out there... But he still wanted to get something. How do you find anything sitting in one place? Someone told him, go to Санаторий [TERM?]... that's what one local area is called... it's quiet there, nobody around, and you'll fill up with loot. Stalkers don't usually go to this Санаторий [TERM?], because there's been nothing useful there for a long time. They were just messing with him. \nSo he took two sacks, got directions from someone, and trudged off. One day gone, two days gone... he only showed up a week later! Turns out he met either some bloodsucker thing or a Burer there, says he barely survived and had to lie low... Probably lying, raising his own price or making excuses because he came back empty. Why make excuses? It was obvious there was nothing to catch there, and the fool fell for it. I haven't been there myself, and I don't feel like wearing out my legs for nothing, so I never even learned the way.

I heard bandits are roaming somewhere around here on the Cordon. What do they need here? One man said they can get from the Cordon to Санаторий [TERM?]. Know anything about that? And from there, maybe somewhere else. I'd like to go see what's so special about this Санаторий [TERM?], just need to find the path... Somebody definitely knows, but that's how it is with us: they find a route and keep quiet like partisans, and if they do say something, it won't be for free.
Heard there was a path to Санаторий [TERM?] somewhere around here? No? And I was just about to ask you... Well, fine, maybe someone knows.
I don't know, I'm very new here myself. Nothing special so far.

I saw a Bloodsucker from a distance three days ago. Well, I mean, they told me it was a Bloodsucker. I looked through binoculars; it looked like a soldier. Is it true they look human? I'm starting to doubt it was a Bloodsucker. Maybe they played me...

I went to the ATP the other day, thought nobody was there. I walk up to the gate and hear some mumbling. I listened closer, and they're talking criminal slang in there. So I thought, to hell with walks like that. I've got a "Макар [TERM?]" under my jacket and cartridges too, but better to keep myself safe. So I turned around and ran back here. Told the men not to poke their noses in for now. You don't go either, wait a while.

Do you have a radiation counter yet? Don't forget to hang it on your belt, or you can step in it badly. I went nearby here once, forgot about the counter at first. Came back, felt awful, the men pointed a device at me, and it started cracking! Everyone shouted: "Take your clothes off, fool!" And I felt sorry for them, the suit was almost new. In the end they stripped me naked by force and threw everything far down into a ravine. Found me some bits of clothing. Then everyone drank vodka to recover, and they made me drink too. My head buzzed for two days. Turns out where I went, there was radioactive dust everywhere. If I hadn't forgotten the counter, I wouldn't be walking around in these rags now.
Last week rain poured for three... no, four days straight. And right nearby, not far from here, there wasn't a drop. They say even the weather has turned anomalous in this Zone.

Boars sometimes run into the village here. So the day before yesterday I was sleeping, heard shooting, thought soldiers had come, was about to jump into the cellar. Looked out, and the boys were chasing Boars. There you go... Be careful, even at the base.

There's an anomaly that can throw you across a distance. I went past the bridge here, walked around a bit, tossed bolts. And that bastard doesn't show up with bolts. I thought, right here it's clear, I'll pass through, and after that the path is already worn in. Then I flew somewhere, went blind at first, then forced my eyes open, holy shit, I'm on some rocks somewhere, and Boars are digging the ground with their snouts all around. Well, I figured out I wasn't far from the base, so I sat quietly for half an hour, waited until they left, then ran from there. That's how it goes in the Zone.

At night there was howling across the whole area. I jumped right up. They say: dogs, don't be scared. And I thought all the dead had risen at once and started screaming, it was that awful. What kind of dogs are those...

During the last emission, I felt so bad, so sick in my soul... This is the third emission I've watched, and every time it gets worse and worse. Maybe it accumulates in a person like radiation, huh? You don't know... I asked the boys, nobody knows.

They told me yesterday: the boys killed a Boar nearby and wanted to cook it. They wonder where to roast it, nobody had matches to start a fire, and they were too lazy to go anywhere far. One of them suggested: "Let's put it near the Burner and throw bolts so it cooks, and we'll turn it over from time to time." So that's what they did. Only the fools don't understand: that's an ANOMALY! Not just a big campfire. How it roasts there, nobody knows. Maybe weak once, strong the next... hell if you can understand it. Five minutes hadn't passed, and the little Boar was nothing but charcoal. That's what you call dinner.

The other day I found an interesting thing near an anomaly. Looked like a gemstone. I showed it to our people, and they were all surprised. Looks exactly like an emerald, they said. In nature, all those stones are found at the sites of old cataclysms too. I mean places where thousands of years ago there was high temperature and pressure... So I thought, maybe here you can collect stones and sell them as precious gems too. There are places like that here. Some kind of loot either way... Need to talk to the traders.

I was coming back from a run, a little tired, got distracted, and caught the edge of an Electro. It slammed me hard! Weapon out of my hands, and I flew off, luckily to the side and not into the center of the anomaly. I came to and saw my "Винторез [TERM?]" lying there, nothing happened to it, but there's no way to reach it. Now I go there three times a day, checking whether it's still there. Waiting for the anomaly to dissipate, since they do change places sometimes. The gun is brand new, I paid so much loot for it... If not for that, would I be standing guard over an anomaly?

We've got one man who deliberately throws different things into anomalies and watches what happens to them. So the other day he threw a grenade into either a Springboard or a Vortex, and it got tossed up, spun around, and torn to pieces. It went off like ten grenades at once. Hell knows, maybe it was some special Vortex, charged up. Everyone grabbed their guns at once, jumped up, thought an emission had hit. We calmed down more or less, and then he came back and told us what he'd done... Fool, in short. Well, no problem, now he won't throw anything for about a week, until the bruises go down. He sees badly out of both eyes now.
Nothing new... Sitting here, sipping vodka while there is some. Well, dogs were running nearby, so we shot them out of boredom. Otherwise, nothing new...

The other day one stalker came back with a lot of loot. Of course he didn't keep it on him, experienced man, hid it in a reliable place. But that's not the point. The point is, people tell the truth: there are anomalous fields that artifacts just love. It's like mushrooms, you understand? In a birch grove you find brown caps, in a pine forest porcini. Lots at once, same here. So it's still worth walking around and looking. Not everything has been picked clean yet...

I'll tell you this: I ran into a Bloodsucker the other day. Nothing new, you always need to be ready for that, especially keep a good shotgun at hand. And as luck would have it, I wasn't ready. I only had an AK and not that many rounds... I went almost light, thought I had piles of ammo in my stash and a shotgun too, why drag extra weight for nothing. Didn't make it. I hear someone huffing. I listened closer, familiar kind of huffing, but nobody visible. I had just cocked the AK when it attacked from behind, knocked the weapon out of my hands, and appeared in all its beauty. I was standing nose to nose with it. My instinct kicked in right away: knife to the throat! Made it. If I'd hesitated a couple seconds, I'd be finished. So a gun is a gun, but always keep a knife ready.

I left supplies in one noticeable place. Came there the other day, and everything was gone, cleaned out completely. Bastards, there really are assholes in this world! If it had been bandits, fine, what do you expect from them, crooks are crooks. But it was our own stalkers who took it, surely. No, assholes like that won't last long in the Zone. I'll kill them myself if I see my own stuff.

I recently shot all kinds of wildlife: Boars, dogs, even winged a Chimera. And I didn't count the Tushkanos at all. Collected all kinds of limbs from them, the ones that were whole or usable, and sold everything to the trader. Came out pretty well. You could basically stop collecting loot and just work monsters, if you have hunting skill. And I do.

Last week a Pseudogiant dragged itself here, and we barely put it down. We had to fuss with it, but all the men were experienced. They climbed higher and pelted it with grenades. Nobody got hurt. With one of those, the main thing is finding a place higher and more reliable, meaning more stable. A big boulder or something like that. God forbid you're standing nearby on the ground when it slaps you with a paw; it'll shake all your guts out at once. Effect is like a head-on car crash... Listen to veterans' stories and take notes.

We went to see the soldiers recently. Turns out you can stock up nicely on ammo and other things from them if you approach them with a business proposal. And they have one need, which means only one offer... guess on the first try what they need, ha-ha.

We were sitting quiet, drinking, singing, having a bite... Then some creep barged in and started throwing his weight around. Well, that was the end of his weight-throwing... Idiot. If he had talked like a human being, fine, but why start flexing right away... who is he to show off in front of us?

Duty members have lost their damn minds, seriously. They keep pushing into the Dark Valley. Who invited them here? Just the other day another smartass showed up... No problem, let him think a little about sticking his nose in without an invitation.

I checked a buddy's stash recently. He left it to me... heh-heh. Must have been storing food for winter or something, there was a mountain of it, the damn Jew. And I kept wondering why he always had new gear. Turns out he was selling food and buying himself kit. Fine by me, now I'll make a profit off his chow.

We were drinking here with the whole crew, and as usual some fool showed up - the turd at the party - and got in Бритый [TERM?]'s face. They locked horns, grabbed their guns, but the men told them right away: want a showdown, fine... knives only, and we'll stand in a circle and watch, no need to ruin our evening... Well, Бритый [TERM?] dropped him, of course, no question. Бритый [TERM?] is a hardened man, and that one was just a kid, I do not even remember his handle. A greenhorn, in short. The piss just went to his head; he sure picked the wrong man to push.
I blew everything at twenty-one... Fuck, what do I do now? No weapon, no ammo - pissed it all away. You can't go into the Zone in just long johns. What an idiot I am...
Nothing special in the news. Chased some mutants around, put down one bandit... Same as always.

Soon they'll shut down this whole racket, I mean the trade in various artifacts. That's the word going around... And good, long overdue. They opened up trade here, spread this contagion around, and the Zone keeps crawling outward.
We're organizing a new recruitment drive. Mostly focused on rookie stalkers, the ones the Zone hasn't ruined too badly yet. Want to serve Duty? Then run to the Bar and sign up.
All the main news is in the Bar, go there. While you're at it, take on some work too; better for the common cause than drifting around and picking up junk all over the Zone.
I don't know, I just got back from a raid. Haven't heard any news yet...

The other day someone wandered in from the Warehouses side. We thought he was just a stalker. But he lingered, asked what was what, where we kept weapons, how many of us were here... The men figured out what kind of bird he was right away. Found himself some fools, the damned anarchist, like we'd tell him everything. He doesn't ask anymore.

There was this case: we were sitting by the campfire at night, all starting to nod off. Suddenly something slapped down nearby, nobody understood at first. Then a canister flew into the wall, and someone's weapon got knocked out of his hands. That's when we realized a Burer had shown up. We scattered fast into position, thought he was about to come for us. Then we looked, and dogs were chasing him across the field. Poor bastard was running from them, barely dodging - a whole pack was after him, and strips were already hanging off him. All he did was throw stones and random junk at them. We even felt sorry for that Burer. But we watched the circus and everyone woke right up.
What news is there, no TV, no radio. Everyone makes something up and serves it as the news of the day... Boring as hell.
"We sat and drank whatever came - Madeira, Starka, St. John's wort..." Know that song? Same with us. That's the news we have, and there isn't any other.

One fool lost his fancy gun while drunk. So we hid it first, then tossed it farther out onto the minefield to see what he would do. He kept walking around whining: where is it, where is it. So we told him: you wandered onto the minefield yourself and left it there, and we pointed out the gun to him... So off he went. The whole base watched his clown show through binoculars, crawling on all fours and probing the ground with a cleaning rod. Some sapper, ha-ha.

One man decided to prank me, told me about a stash like I would take the bait. I know those jokes. I agreed, then offered it to one rookie instead. So I watched from a hill through my scope as he searched that stash, crawling to it on all fours. It was mined, well, not for real, just a smoke popper. Oh, how he jumped and squealed... we had a good laugh.
Mutants came pushing in from the Барьер [TERM?] side, and we barely stopped them. Where do they come from over there? Maybe someone breeds them on purpose, haven't heard?

We had a newcomer show up recently. So we laughed at him a little. He got wiped out hard from not being used to it - who drinks that much right away with no food - and at night one man gave him a whole set of hickeys. We have a fellow here who likes that kind of thing. In the morning we started telling him how he got drunk and went to the Bloodsucker village, chasing them around and yelling: "Come on out, we're going to fight!" Then we pointed at the hickeys. The kid almost shit himself... And we had a laugh.

Duty members dropped by, thought we just drink here and could be taken barehanded. Like hell! Sure, we were a little drunk, but for me that only helps - steadier hand and sharper eye, if you keep it moderate. Dropped two of them, and the rest ran back to the Bar quick. No need to stick their noses in here.

I saw one kid's gun. The grip was done up with some kind of plexiglass, and under it was an oil painting or something like that. Pretty, but what the hell is it for... To me the main thing is that it shoots straight. Decorating it like that is just showing off.

We found a door here, well, an entrance somewhere, hell knows where. We tried forcing it and forcing it, nothing. Welded shut, maybe, or locked from the other side... Maybe that's where those creeps from Duty come from? Too many of them around here...

One man here lost everything at dice and now he whines: give me this, give me that... Why the hell was he playing high stakes with someone else's dice? Fool. If you're betting big, you need your own dice. A lesson for a sucker like him...

The other day we caught a stalker with loot on him. What a sap, carrying it all with him... So he started whining: I'll give it all up, just let me go. Sure you'll give it up, and you'll work it off too if you want to live. So he did a little mule work for us: we gave him a basic gun and sent him where we needed. Worked out fine - he shot some doggies, some Tushkanos. We kept him in our sights, of course, so he only pointed the gun the right way. He cleared the area for us, though not all of it... didn't give him many rounds, heh-heh. And to hell with him, plenty of fools still walking around.
The boss has been in a bad mood lately. I went to him, and he tore me up good... What's got into him?
Nothing new, same work as always.
Who are you that I should tell you the news? Keep walking.
No news, and even if there was, you wouldn't hear it.
We don't like chatter here. And we don't like people who ask too many questions.
If we're still tolerating you here, that doesn't mean you can do whatever you want. Don't ask too much. Better yet, walk around silent.
You're still alive. That's news already...
What news? Open your mouth less, especially here. Walk while you still can, just keep quiet.
You're still here? Haven't gathered all the news yet? Come on, get out of here.

Can you play dice? Come on, stop acting like a good little boy. You probably already played at the Bar with Baldy... So I'll tell you this: don't deal with him, he's one hell of a bastard. Recently he cleaned out Пашка [TERM?] so badly everyone stood there with their mouths open - how can the same number come up three times in a row? And the man had just come back from a raid. Found something valuable, naturally... He had a lot of loot, too. I warned him the same way: don't deal with Baldy. But no... And now what? He walks around the Bar like the last bum, and he had that much loot! Ah. Fool.

Yesterday at the Bar I lost twenty grand to Baldy. Didn't play anymore after that... Something about his luck smells wrong. And tell me... you look like a stalker, sort of... where does he get artifacts he can put on the table so casually? He sits in the Bar, never goes anywhere... So where from? Nobody knows.

Sometimes people play in the Bar here. Haven't tried it yet? You put down money - he answers with an artifact. Then it depends how the die turns, meaning what number comes up. The rules aren't hard, only the whole thing is strange... I think Baldy - he's the man who stakes artifacts - is somehow in cahoots with Barkeep. Because Barkeep buys arts cheap from rookies. He sees your face looks like a rookie's, and that's it, consider yourself skinned clean. Then he somehow rents those artifacts to Baldy, Baldy makes his profit and pays Barkeep back in money. That's what we talked over among ourselves and decided. Otherwise it doesn't add up, Baldy having both money and arts.
They beat me again in the Bar. Those fuckers... They're doing something crooked with the bets there. Fine, we'll still tune up Baldy's face...

Ah... I'm an idiot. They warned me, didn't they: don't stake arts, stake money. I pissed it all away. And if I had sold them, imagine where I'd be now. I had almost a hundred grand there, no less... What an idiot.
Get lost! Demob is right around the corner... and after that! I'll live properly...
Move along... Know too much, and no one will ever find you...

You should be more careful... nearby, at the pumping station, some brave little officer set up a secret little oil refinery, picked soldiers as guards - real cutthroats, and their faces... compared to them, mutants are cartoon animals. I'm afraid they might do something stupid while drunk. So keep an eye out.

My colleagues and I decided to set up a bathhouse at the former boat station. After dissecting certain representatives of the local fauna, you get so saturated with the smell that I fear some stalkers may soon start shooting at our kind in the dark.

Most curious people turn up, I must say. Yesterday they brought in one such case... he had two molecules of life left in him, but he kept asking about some album of drawings and tugging at the amulet on his chest... We thought he would die, even wanted to call Father Diodor, but when we woke up in the morning, he was gone without a trace... Miracles...
You go on your way. I don't know anything...
I don't know. Seems like nothing. Ask around, maybe someone will tell you something.
What could be new in the Zone, think about it yourself? Same monsters, same anomalies, same faces every day.
I learned one tricky little recipe. Tried it, and it worked. Turns out it's all so simple, while at first I couldn't figure out what was what at all.
Where would we get news? We sit here on the edge of nowhere. If someone tells us something from somewhere, we're the last to hear it.

I don't know anything. Some people go far, then tell all kinds of interesting stories, and I just sit here and listen. They tell me: why are you sitting here, at least go to Garbage. I'll get there. The slower you go, the farther you'll get.

There was an incident the other day: bandit scum came pouring in by the line. Where did so many of them come from? And they push in brazenly, don't give a damn about anything. They don't even care whether they live or die. What kind of people are they! We fought them off in the end, but it's a shame about our boys. They were good men.

As usual, one of the stalkers ended up in an anomaly. How many times have people said it: you can't run around the Zone without a head on your shoulders. First probe the path with a bolt, then go. But no! Always in a hurry... never enough time for them.

Well, want me to tell you a few words about the Burer? Have you even seen one? When you do, you'll know right away it's him. Your head starts humming and all kinds of objects start flying. And God forbid you meet his eyes! The moment you spot him from a distance, hide. If he sees you, he'll suck your whole soul out through his stare.

Know about Bloodsuckers? When you're walking anywhere, always listen. That's how you have to move everywhere in the Zone. You have to listen to the Zone. So, as soon as you sense someone huffing nearby - even a hint of huffing - that's it! Run, or you won't make it. Well, if you're tough and carrying a huge gun, you can face him. Only he's such a bastard, he can come from behind, you won't even notice, and then no gun will help you. Better to run right away.

I know now how to deal with Snorks. Truth is, I haven't run into one yet, thank God. But experienced stalkers told me: you can sense a Snork right away, it's too noisy. The noise gives it away. As soon as you hear that kind of... guttural sound, maybe, it means he's nearby. Best aim for the head. And the main thing is don't gape, the bastard is very quick. If he jumps at you, move aside at once and shoot. Better yet, hit him in midair, but still move aside right away, then finish him. That's theory, of course. I haven't had practice myself yet, but it is still better to know what to do in advance.

I recently went out on some business, and those... little ones attacked me. Tushkanos. What nasty things! Like locusts. They'll gnaw anyone to death, small as they look. With them, the main thing is to jump onto something high right away - a rock, a car, a fallen tree. And while they swarm around down below, calmly pick them off with a pistol. Only you need to aim carefully, because they're so quick you can spend your whole supply and still not hit a single one.

One stalker said people had seen dogs that weren't really real, like an illusion. Supposedly something like a Pseudodog runs at you, you shoot, and it disappears, then runs again. What the hell is that? True, or are they lying? In the Zone, anything can happen...

They say the rats running around here sometimes are mutating too. Someone saw not a rat, but a damn giant rat! Like a dog. And they also say rats attacked one stalker and ate him alive, while the second barely got away. He told the story afterward. Terrible things are happening.

Dogs ate one stalker recently. He kept bragging: I can do this, I have that! Said he had an assault rifle, and they were just brainless dogs. So he bragged himself into trouble - ran into such a pack he didn't even have time to change magazines. Exactly, they're brainless, they don't understand they're being shot. And while you're shooting one, ten more are already chewing your legs... Braggarts like that don't live long in the Zone.

Something happened with the army boys at Agroprom. They say helicopters were flying, looked like some bigwig came in... for an inspection. Probably someone stole something as usual, or they lost something, who knows... Just in case: don't show yourself there too much, that's my advice.

People are saying the military started stirring at Agroprom... I don't really care, but they say there was some kind of mess there. Or maybe just a rotation... or whatever they call it, one group gets taken out, another brought in, and all the fuss comes from that.

I heard the army boys don't stand on ceremony with stalkers much anymore. Rumor says some clever bastard got into their Agroprom base... though I honestly didn't understand where, the base itself or the database. Not the point... something went missing from them, in short. An acquaintance told me that, and a soldier whispered it to him, warned him, basically, so our stalkers would watch themselves around there for a while.

The army boys have some kind of reinforcements here on the Cordon. And a man from Agroprom dropped by too, said it's the same there. They're planning something, or maybe someone ran off... or got killed... Just guesses. We're sitting here wondering what brought it on. So be even more careful with them now, because who knows what's on their minds and what new orders they've received...

Agroprom has been restless lately for some reason. I'm sitting here and not going out for now. I almost stuck my nose out, wanted to crawl to the Cordon, then heard some serious shooting. Something is clearly happening. I think I'll sit it out here until everything calms down. I only just got back on my feet, and now to catch a bullet... why would I need that kind of joy.

You went to the Dark Valley, didn't you... Better tell me yourself what they're saying there. One stalker said some fool either got into a sealed laboratory or was about to. He lied, of course... who would risk that? And if it's true, I wouldn't want to be in his place. Once, back when the entrance was almost open, our people tried to poke in there and barely got out alive - so much wildlife in there. If you learn anything, tell me...

Seems they brought Barkeep something unusual. A guard heard Barkeep talking with some smart types, something about technical documentation, some laboratory... What could he even be saying there? He's no technician. What does he know about documentation... But I'd be interested in looking through those docs myself.

Stalkers wounded one bandit. So, to get them to let him go, he started telling fairy tales about a cache in a laboratory nearby. Not up top either, but down below, in the laboratory itself, imagine that. They told him: what are you feeding us, there's no way in there! But he swore someone found a key and even went there. Haven't heard anything? The cache is bullshit, of course, but could there really be a passage now?

There was a stalker from the Dark Valley here right before you, started talking all kinds of nonsense about an underground laboratory. I don't know why stalkers suddenly got interested in laboratories again... Those laboratories really do exist. I remember back when the Clear Sky group was working, there was a lot of talk about them. There were different plans for them, but only the leadership knew what, how, and why. Then everyone went quiet, and now there's suddenly some real excitement again.

What news would we have here, just echoes of gossip. A stalker came by recently and spun such a tale: supposedly they found a key to some underground laboratory and someone even went there... True, he didn't say whether the man came back. And it was obvious anyway: of course he didn't come back, otherwise why would he be telling it? Clear enough he made it up himself. I say all that is stalker folklore. Laboratories are to stalkers what castle dungeons are, with their secrets and dead men guarding those secrets. That's why I say nobody could have come back, otherwise he'd come out and say: it's all fairy tales, boys, don't believe any of it...

I heard a rumor that some underground place was opened somewhere. And supposedly everything there died out... or everyone died out... and you can find a lot of interesting things. Worth believing, what do you think? If everything and everyone died out, what's so interesting there? Though... some junk probably stayed behind.

I recently went out to the stalkers to get some food, and they told me monsters came crawling out of some underground levels... supposedly some idiot blew open a door down there. Look at what is happening! And I'm sitting here and know nothing...

Now we have a group at Yantar too, guarding the scientists. Why guard them, they're in a bunker... we'd better survive ourselves. Do you know how much filth is there! Dead men walking like they're alive. They say someone opened some lower levels there... I don't know properly, but it is certain that all kinds of filth crawled out of there. You haven't been there by chance? Seen those dead men? If you go, help our boys; they say they're having a rough time there now, not like here.

They say things are lively at Yantar. Duty even assigned some of their own people there. One man came here to get repaired after duty, said he nearly went gray there. Shot so much his barrel could have melted. He didn't stop until he spent all his ammo; they barely calmed him down. And it all started recently, after someone went into the territory of that... uh... X-lab. Looks like he stirred up those creatures, and they came in a wave. But that's just my guess. Ask around at Yantar about what's what.

Duty has another problem now: Yantar. One Duty member came by recruiting for their ranks. Started talking pretty about defending humanity, this and that... But once he drank, his tongue loosened. He said they actually don't have enough people, and now there is duty at Yantar too. Mostly volunteers get sent there, but there aren't many takers. Someone opened either a hatch or a passage there... and the monsters that had been locked up sort of smelled freedom, then all rushed outside. So that's why nobody is eager to go there: it's become very dangerous.

I heard a rumor that someone really did visit the laboratories after all... at Yantar, maybe, or somewhere there. I doubt it's true, because even in our time everything was sealed tight there and all the passages were blocked. Though if someone really wants in, he'll find a way. Anything can happen. Fanat recently asked someone to go to a laboratory specifically for some documents, and he offered it to me too... Actually, he's the one who told me about it. Only nobody agreed. We aren't suicides here, and I don't need it that badly.

What news do we have? We ask everyone ourselves. The other day someone came to Сергеич [TERM?], seemed to be from the scientists at Yantar. I was just bringing them food. They were mumbling something about their research, fluc... what do you call it... tuations or something, and then I hear Сергеич [TERM?] get surprised by something. Laboratory, he says, radiation? Can't be, he says, for it to be turned off, just the power, he says, is variable... At that point I stopped understanding anything and left, so I wouldn't clog my head with it. So what was that about? You don't know either... shame.

Rumor has it there will soon be a major scrap between the Duty grunts and Freedom. Freedom, supposedly, had its eye on the passage to Yantar, so the Duty grunts hurried up and they're already there. One man was coming from the Bar and told me. He says the Duty grunts deliberately released zombies from some underground levels at Yantar so that, if it came to it, Freedom would waste all its strength on them. Like some kind of barrier. Only a "barrier" like that can come back to bite them. You can't stuff ideals into zombies' heads; they don't care who they kill.

I don't know anything. I feel more or less all right now, need to get to the Bar. At least they'll tell me something there. Otherwise, everyone who comes by tells how dead men from the complex at Yantar moved out in a crowd. That was after Duty members made a raid there. Seems that way... You don't know what to believe, I need to go to the Bar myself and ask around.

People here say Duty members are getting stronger. They've gotten completely brazen, want to grab everything for themselves. Something happened at Yantar - someone switched something on or off, hell if you can understand... so under that cover they quickly found both funds and men and already dug in there. And now they think it's their lawful territory. Same thing happened in the Dark Valley. Someone went somewhere there, let somebody out of a cage, and there they were at once, starting to "protect" all humanity from the spawn of the Zone. They probably encouraged it themselves so they'd have a "legal" excuse for a takeover. Fine... let them have their fun. We won't let them in here, and we won't let them pass farther either.

I was just about to ask you... Someone told me mutants came in a wave, not here, but in the Dark Valley, and at Yantar too. They blew something up there, I think... a passage or an exit, I don't really know. What I do know is that we may get more work now: shooting monsters, if that wave rolls this far.

I'm not much interested in all these stalker rumors of yours. One comes in and says the army boys lost something, another comes in and says monsters crawled out of the underground, a third says the radiation, or whatever it is, got turned off... None of that matters to me. The main thing is that with those rumors, stalkers also bring good loot. If they only come in to chatter, I don't even listen.
They say some artifacts can drive a person mad... Only I haven't met anyone who really lost his mind because of an art.

I heard people sometimes find strange stones. Like there's a radio built into them. You find a stone like that and listen to it, and it tells you what's happening around the area. Someone, they say, was even shown the way to the center of the Zone. But it's a rare thing, you won't just find one.

Heard about cobblestones? People have recipes for transforming artifacts. So when an artifact like that passes through the final stage of transformation, it turns into a cobblestone, but not an ordinary one, a charmed one. And with that cobblestone, you can then pass wherever you want...

I heard the Zone was formed when a meteorite struck the ground. And on impact, the meteorite exploded. Its main mass remained there in the center, while the blast scattered fragments all over the Zone, and now people find those fragments. They aren't artifacts, of course, but you can still sell them, especially to scientists.

If you find an unusual stone that makes your head start humming, don't even think about keeping it on you. I heard someone picked up one like that, carried it for a while, then lost it. After that his head started humming even worse and still hums to this day. People start talking to him, and it's like he doesn't hear them, just says "huh, what?" - that's how loud the hum is in his head.

There's a rumor going around that some artifacts sort of talk to you. Whoever carries them starts rambling little by little... At first just a bit, then they won't shut their mouth - always saying something to someone, but when you look, there's nobody nearby. That's them talking to the artifact.

So many schizos have cropped up in the Zone! And the farther in, the more of them. One mumbles something about the Monolith, another says he's expected at the center of the Zone, a third starts spouting bullshit about his own uniqueness. And the most broken ones are those who start talking to themselves, then throw themselves into anomalies.

Heard about the guy who said he was "chosen"? Someone told me recently. He was a normal man, then found either some cobblestone or just a rock, and his roof slid off. Started telling everyone he was chosen by the Zone, that he had an impossible mission and he alone could carry it out. The things the Zone does to people!

They say someone finds meteorite fragments. So those fragments, after lying in anomalies, take on strange properties: they affect people like some kind of hypnotic device. You don't even notice when you fall under their influence. They tell you: kill your comrade, it has to be done! And the person carrying such a fragment goes and kills.

Heard about the meteorite fragments? Bullshit. It wasn't a meteorite at all. I was told those are fragments of the Monolith itself! In one powerful emission the Monolith exploded, only nothing happened to it, a few fragments just flew off, and that's all. Zombies picked up those fragments and started carrying them around the Zone. And whoever now meets such a zombie with a fragment becomes a zombie himself and takes that burden from him, to carry it farther.

They say "Чёрная метка [TERM?]" is a cooled fragment of the Monolith itself. When it crawled up from underground, well, or from somewhere, it caught fire at first and started crackling like a heated stone. Those fragments flew off from that, and as they cooled, they lost weight. And they became, well, like dandelion seeds - the wind carried them all over the Zone. And whoever picked up such a seed of the Monolith got that same "Чёрная метка [TERM?]."
Same as always. We shoot wildlife bit by bit. Enough to live on.
They say someone saw Burers here. If it's true, that's bad news. How did they wander here, and most importantly, from where?
They say there are so many dogs beyond the bridge that it's better not to poke that way alone. These dogs breed here like... cats.

Here, somewhere to the north, they spotted a pack of cats... or whatever cats have, a pride, devil knows, a lot of them in short. And there never used to be any. Well, fine - we'll make hats for winter.

A rookie showed up here recently. You could tell right away: some engineer, not a hunter. Started putting forward his efficiency proposals: let's specialize, he says, divide duties, someone hunts Tushkanos, someone hunts Boars... City smartass. If I only go after little Tushkanos, how much will I make off them? Just wasted rounds. And he, meaning, will shoot Fleshes, the most profitable beast in our parts. The men quickly explained what's what around here... Haven't seen him since. Guess he didn't like that arrangement.

Everything's the same here... Different people come through. Only not everyone can hunt. They think it's that simple: "bang-bang" and done. Like hell... you need skill here, need to know the beast's habits. Doesn't matter that it mutated; a beast stays a beast.

Recently some businesslike type showed up. Looked like one of those former chairmen, some communard. He says: "Let's live as a commune and split everything equally." This patch, he says, is Vanka's, that patch is Sanka's, that one over there is Petka's... We say: "And who the hell are you? Where's your patch?" Mine, he says, is beyond the bridge. Businesslike, right?! That's the best hunting ground there. Ignat put him in his place right away. Damn communard! We should have crushed more of them back in the day.

Heard about Dan? He used to be here. Then he and Ignat couldn't divide something. I don't know what happened between them, only Dan left for the Cordon somewhere. A few more guys went with him. And Ignat pretends everything is fine: he wanted to leave, so he left... A shady story.

Some gopniks came to Ignat recently. They talked something over between themselves, and then Ignat gathered everyone and said: we need to shoot a lot of Fleshes, big order for hides. Yeah... We had a good hunt the other day, didn't spare ammo - Ignat handed it out generously. Only the payment is unclear... He's quiet for now. Need to find out from him what's what...
Met some egghead here recently. He had such businesslike gear - a special suit, like a spacesuit. What are they doing here? This is practically the outskirts...

They say the men on the Cordon take tribute from stalkers. True, they call it contributions in favor of hunters, but whatever you call it, it's extortion. I heard Ignat gets something from it, like he has a share. Some tricky relationship they've got there.

I heard that after an emission, wildlife starts breeding. Like some rut begins in them right after it. How else do you explain their numbers? No matter how many we kill, they never run out and never run out...

They say stalkers call wildlife monsters... Is that so? What's so scary about them? They told me about the Bloodsucker, and yes, that one is probably a monster... I haven't met one myself. The rest are ordinary beasts. Same pigs, just twisted a little, same Boars. Dogs are just dogs. And Tushkanos are like rats, only bigger.

Tried our sausage? Someone whispered to me recently that besides Flesh and Boar meat, they add dog meat too, supposedly for piquancy. It's meat too, of course, but I prefer to think it's still only pork... And we also have gourmets who smoke Tushkano legs with spices, so you can't tell them from chicken legs. Same thing, just tougher.

I heard Ignat ordered a whole batch of tin cans from the mainland, and some kind of device, like a mini-autoclave. Looks like we'll be making canned food ourselves now. Sidorovich will be "thrilled" about that competition.
I heard all kinds of people keep pestering others with stupid questions: "What have I heard?" So get lost...

Sidorovich has gotten completely fat and greedy! Someone says to him: "Here, take an artifact," and he turns his nose up: not the right one, he says. No, just look at this piece of work. No decency in him! Businessman...

Васька [TERM?] found a lighter in some house here recently. Old enough to remember Брежнев [TERM?]. And it works. Doesn't run out either, imagine that! He thought life was sweet now, savings and all. Then at the checkpoint he gave Kuznetsov a light... Handed it right into his hands! What a scatterbrain, huh? Well, at least during Kuznetsov's shift he gets through quietly, and that's something.

Санёк Лохматый [TERM?] bit a dog to death recently, heard that? Oh, pal, that was something. In the evening he went behind the bushes to count the stars, and then we hear someone scream! We run over and see Sanyok lying there, shit all over himself, and a blind dog on top of him. While he was counting stars, he says, he dropped his assault rifle beside him. Then he started feeling around and realized what was in his hand wasn't the rifle at all. When that something jerked toward him, he sank his teeth into it. And I don't think the dog died from fright at all. It was just old, that's what I say.
In the Dark Valley, some asshole sells ammo. Cheap, but those rounds are deadly if they hit you on the head from the twelfth floor... just swing harder when you throw them.

I was at Garbage. Looks like the bandit scum got their heads beaten in hard; now it's quiet and peaceful in the hangar and at the vehicle graveyard. Duty members are good men... they can do it when they want to!

Sanyok told me this: he was in the Swamps. Says he is trudging along, not bothering anyone. Then he sees a fisherman sitting by a puddle. All hunched up, holding a rod, doesn't care about a thing, like he is somewhere in Подмосковье [TERM?]. Figures the man got tired of all this mess, missed fishing, and Sanyok likes that too, so he walked up to him. Says: buddy, around here you might catch a crocodile with elephant ears, but fish are a lost cause... The man turns around, and it's a dead man! Moaning, gurgling, waving some stick around. Sanyok talked and talked at him, and the thing just sat there. What kind of filth did that to a man, huh? Looks like he died, but see how it still twists him... that's how it is.

Why are you bothering me? What am I supposed to tell you when the only news here is another report about another stiff. Wait for the newspaper "Голос снорка [TERM?]" to come out; they'll tell you everything there, even with pictures.

Listen, this is impossible already. I stashed some loot recently, thought the place was perfect! Nobody would find it. But no, they got in there too! Next time I'm putting only mines in my stash. I'm sick of those rat bastards, I hate them!

Some scarecrow dragged in a stone here recently. The stone shone like it was greased, and this sap starts bragging: "Look how lucky I am! Unknown artifact! I'll rake in piles of cash." He sat there with that little stone in his hands, and then - bam! He got sick. Where's the exit here, he says, I can't seem to find it. Серёга Черешня [TERM?] is more experienced than most of us, so he sensed right away something was wrong, grabbed his dosimeter fast, and the scale wasn't enough! You scrawny bastard, he yells, everyone get out of here now! Turns out this clown was wandering around with no dosimeter, found that little stone everyone avoided, because it was practically glowing, fuck it! Dragged it to us, the bastard. Can't go into that house now, and my gear is still in there...

You know what I don't understand? Where are people's heads? They come here like it's a picnic. Guitars, songs by the fire... Last week someone ate three tourists like that. They probably didn't even have time to understand anything, let alone shoot. Fools, damn it...

Once I was walking toward a Duty checkpoint, and I hear noise, chaos, Duty men yelling, calling everyone nearby for help because wildlife is overwhelming them! So I run, spit flying in the wind, get there, and everything is squealing, grunting, and barking. I get to work: threw every grenade I had, my rifle was practically smoking, some bastard bit through my hand, still aches... And one asshole over there, their warrant officer, says: "You're weak, no use at all"... can you imagine? I tell him: buddy, is that a joke, or are you seriously saying my help isn't worth a sniff? I say, I carry an F-1 grenade for special occasions, and for something like this I'll gift it to you right now down your collar with the pin out... Bastard. He wilted fast, the ugly mug, and now lets me into the Bar without a sound.

People say the army boys at Agroprom got robbed. Some slick bastard stole documents from them. It's boiling over there now, holy hell! A commission arrived, and the local captain flew to the mainland... looks like not by helicopter, but by a boot up the ass. No point sticking your nose in there now; they'll drop you at once.

Heard stalkers recently rescued someone at Agroprom? The army boys pinned them down. They say a fair number of people died. What a mess is going on, and why can't people live quietly? Are we at war or what? We didn't come here to fight a war... We're already walking the edge, and if we start shooting each other too, that's disaster.

Have you ever led tourists around these parts? No? Always an adventure with them. Someone told me: Митяй Длинный [TERM?] got mixed up with them once, because they asked very nicely. Very, very nicely... So he got the job of escorting two clowns into... in short, watching over them, yes. So there they are, walking slowly, Митяй [TERM?] tosses stones and bolts into anomalies for them, shows them a Tushkano from a distance, the standard Zone safari package, right. Then he sees one of them fidgeting, dancing in place. He says: "Why are you dancing? Need to go?" The man flashes his little glasses, like yes, that's the issue. Митяй [TERM?] crawled through the bushes first, checked it was all clear. Go fertilize the Zone, he says...\n They stand and wait. He tells the other one: "Keep the rifle ready, friend, and stand with your back to me, or you'll fire from fright, empty head with ears." Then they hear a voice - the shitter is mumbling something: hello, he says, allow me to introduce myself, I am so-and-so, associate professor of the department... They go to him. And they see this egghead has already pulled up his pants and is standing there talking to a Snork! The thing must have fallen asleep or something... unclear. Митяй [TERM?] puts one into its skull right away before it wakes up - bang! And that little shitter screams: "Murderer, criminal, madman!" Then the second one catches up: "What have you done, this is a crime, you can't kill the mentally ill, this is some kind of fascism"... Митяй [TERM?] didn't get it at first, then figured it out: they mistook the Snork for a psycho in a gas mask. He led them closer: look, he says, see what kind of little animal this is. They started puking right away!.. But after that, he says, they walked quiet as mice, step after step. Eggheads...

Heard a mutant has appeared in the Zone? Looks completely human from the outside. But appearances deceive, right? So this scarecrow walks up to everyone he meets and asks: "What new can you tell me?" You'll give him the news, remember every last piece of gossip, even make new ones up just to appease that ghoul, and he still keeps hammering: "What new can you tell me?.." You haven't run into that mutant by chance, have you?

They say there's a lake called Yantar here. Nothing but zombies wandering around there, in crowds... Walking and moaning. They said those zombies gather in hangars, walk through factory workshops, sit in broken sheds. And they also said that by that lake there's a mobile bunker, and it's been standing there since the first time everything blew. And zombies sit there in white coats, from back then, can you imagine? Everything proper: guards walk around, those little lab coats run out to take samples, worms have eaten everything out of them, but they still twitch there and wander around... Horror!

Everything's rotten... On the Cordon they caught one little creep - a quick type, here one moment, there the next... Filled our ears with lies, the little nightingale. If you see him, send greetings from the crew. We remember him...
What can I say, we're dying out... Zone here, Zone there. The cops in choppers are killing our crew, and beasts roam in herds. Everyone's so sharp, so lively...

Been to Garbage? In short, some shady outsiders rolled up on the drifters there, and their boss is a devil... or what's his name - Bes, you know him? He made a claim, like: you clueless riffraff, clear out while it's quiet, we'll hold the line here and manage without you. The boys didn't understand: there was no agreement, and they weren't talking by the code... Then those brutes pulled out guns and put almost everyone down. Scumbags... We'll straighten that devil's horns for him...

Once the boys and I were standing around, and some creep comes striding toward us from the forest edge, a brazen moose of a man, antlers to the sky, not a care in the world. And Knysh [TERM?] is a serious man, so he shouts to him: "Wait up, buddy. You're walking down this road, but you don't know that the boys guarding this path have a hard time sometimes."\n That moose puffed up at first, stood there glaring, then opened his mouth and shouted at us, like, boys, I have no cash, no food, I'm not local, and please let me go, sirs. Knysh [TERM?] tells him: "Nobody is holding you. Why did you panic? Go on your way... Only the crew asks you to share what you can, out of the kindness of your heart. We're not robbing you like snot-nosed street punks, you're hurting our feelings." So he takes the sack off his back and drags out a little wheel of sausage. Knysh [TERM?] takes it and says: "Thanks, man, only here's the trouble: there are this many of us here, and this isn't enough... Look, Kolyan's [TERM?] boots are completely shot, and you've got real good combat boots, and that cloak is decent too, so share a little. You'll find yourself more gear, you're a lucky man, anyone can see that"...\n And that moose forces out a grin and shouts: "Now you've got luck too - yours, forever." Knysh [TERM?] swelled up a bit, and we look at his hands, and it's not sausage there, but some black piece of shit, like a drop! Knysh [TERM?] starts squealing something about a mark, we aim to drop that creep, but he's gone already, imagine that! Vanished like the earth swallowed him. Only Knysh [TERM?] started rushing around like a cockroach, then flew straight into an anomaly, even though we'd walked that spot a hundred times... That's the kind of mess it was, buddy.
Found yourself a storyteller, have you? Want me to sing too, and dance? Sure, I can, just whistle...
Leave me alone... Some filth cut up a friend of mine recently. He had a decent rifle. Some creature stabbed him at night. Ah...
There is such a phenomenon as mutant psi-influence on humans. Have you ever experienced anything like it? Yes? Most unpleasant sensations, wouldn't you agree?

Last week I was taking measurements... And imagine, something came over me that cannot be explained from a scientific point of view. My vision suddenly went dark and... I saw a pack of rats. A huge pack. Everything was swarming with them. Then it let me go, I looked, and there were no rats, never had been. What it means, I do not understand.

Ever run into Burers? They say they're like hamsters: they drag anything they find into their burrow. And whatever doesn't fit, they carry around with them. The men here said they gutted one of those Burer storehouses. What a fight that was - there was all kinds of stuff in there! Canned food, boots, ammo, not counting empty junk. Where they gathered it all, who knows... Only who the hell needs that around here. Well, the smarter men waited until all kinds of fools smashed each other's noses over the junk, then went for the real loot... They grabbed a decent haul, they say. Lied about half of it, of course, maybe more, but some of it must be true...
Heard they saw a woman somewhere? Around the Swamps, supposedly... I feel like taking a walk. It's not like I really need it, but sometimes you want to squeeze something warm and springy a little...

You didn't see what happened here... Some kid came running. Still far off, he yells: "Bloodsucker! Watch out, crew!" So we scattered wherever we could, hid and watched... And this transparent mug runs up to Диман копчёный [TERM?]. He'd dozed off a little after yesterday and sat down by the gate. And chomp! Bit Диман [TERM?]'s head off. We stood there with our mouths open, and it tucked what was left under its arm and ran from us. We still didn't really figure out what it was... a Bloodsucker, or some other crap that appeared.
On the hill a fellow stands, and he cannot pass the land: over him a "Whirligig," all around the "Jelly" big, then a Chimera came to play, start the fairy tale again...

We had one junkie, a terrible murky type, always walking around wasted. He preyed on stalkers who were crawling half-dead, barely alive. Watched for them on the paths, saw someone and right away: "Buddy, let me help. Ah, look how badly you're twisted up," while he gave him a little kiss with a knife and stole his things. Pure butcher, dark soul. Nobody went on jobs with him, too nasty, and anyway...\n So he would have kept rustling around by himself. Then one day he came back covered in blood. Shaking, moaning. He had his own corner where he cooked his junk and food, always hid there. And then you could hear him mumbling there. A junkie is a junkie, people like that always grumble something, but this one sounded like he was talking to someone, like he was making excuses. Then he screamed: "Go away! Go away, I killed you already." Enough! The boys and I kicked him outside, he yelled out there and went quiet... In the morning the crew shouts: that junkie hanged himself on a poplar, and so neatly he was right at the top. How he climbed up there, hell knows. That's the kind of crap it was...
